# commentable-html feature specification

commentable-html turns a standalone HTML artifact into a self-contained code-review surface. A reader can select text, code, diff lines, mermaid nodes or whole diagrams, headings, images, and charts, leave comments in the page, copy a machine-readable review bundle back to an agent, and let the agent mark processed comments handled in the same HTML file.

The core invariant is self-contained operation. Standalone mode embeds the CSS, state blocks, UI, and runtime in one file. NonPortable mode keeps the document-owned state inline but loads CSS and JS from local companion files in `dist/`; its export action rebuilds one portable file so the review can travel without companions. The review layer is bundled into the document. Optional host features, such as mermaid rendering, Chart.js, tooltip libraries, and Run in Azure Data Explorer links, can load from a CDN and should be vendored, inlined, or captured with Export Offline when single-file zero-network portability is required.

Current UI label note: the authoritative action label is `Export as Portable`. Some historical test names still say `Save comments`, `Save in HTML`, or `Export with embedded comments`.

Coverage notation: each row lists the strongest automated coverage or source citation I found. `manual/validator` means the behavior is a documented convention or a static validator invariant rather than a browser E2E behavior test.

| CMH-STATE-01 | Widget layout changes are tracked without being comments: moving a part surfaces a non-comment layout-change card and flips the badge to Not portable, Copy all adds a `Widget layout changes` section, moving a part back clears it and restores Portable, moves to `(no slot)` and post-load removals are reported while post-load additions are not, the same part id in two widgets is tracked independently, the sidebar auto-opens once on the first change, and the state card carries no `data-cid` (its only actions are `state-jump`/`state-reset`, never editable or deletable as a comment), keeps the comment count at 0, and never enters `HANDLED_IDS`. | `tests/41-widget-statediff.spec.js` - full layout-diff suite; source: `assets/js/` - `widgetStateChanges()` |
| CMH-BOARD-01 | A moved `[data-cm-widget][data-cm-draggable]` board grows a runtime-injected, `cm-skip` "Reset moves" button (title "Return cards to their original positions") whenever its layout differs from the load-time baseline; clicking it restores every part to its original slot and order, and the button disappears when there are no moves. Static (non-draggable) widgets never get the button. | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `a moved draggable board grows a Reset moves button that restores it (CMH-BOARD-01)`, `the Reset moves button is only for draggable boards, not static widgets (CMH-BOARD-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `resetWidgetMoves()`, `_syncWidgetResetButtons()` |
| CMH-BOARD-02 | The sidebar renders one layout-change state card per widget that has changes, each with a `state-jump` button that scrolls to that board and flashes it, and a "Reset changes" button that restores only that widget's parts. | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `each changed board gets its own state card with jump and Reset changes (CMH-BOARD-02)`, `a state card jump focuses and flashes its own board (CMH-BOARD-02)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_renderWidgetStateCard()`, `_jumpToWidget()` |
| CMH-BOARD-03 | A state card mirrors a regular comment card's shape: an `in: <board>` title (the widget aria-label, else its name), a jump button, and a meta line showing the first-change datetime via `formatTime()`, alongside the existing explanatory note. | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `a state card carries an in:<board> title, jump, and a first-change timestamp (CMH-BOARD-03)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_renderWidgetStateCard()`, `widgetFirstChangeAt()` |
| CMH-BOARD-04 | Clear restores every tracked draggable board move to its authored load-time layout, removes the state card, and returns the document badge to Portable when no other review state remains. | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `Clear restores draggable board moves to the authored baseline (CMH-BOARD-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `resetAllWidgetMoves()`, Clear handler |
| CMH-BOARD-05 | Clear restores boards that opt into drag through slot-level `data-cm-draggable`, restores each changed board's exact load-time sibling order including interleaved non-part elements, and does not mutate clean draggable boards. | `tests/58-board-ux.spec.js` - `Clear restores slot-level draggable boards in exact order without touching clean boards (CMH-BOARD-05)`; source: `assets/js/` - `resetAllWidgetMoves()`, `_snapshotWidgetState()` |
| CMH-DOCCMT-01 | Right-clicking empty space (fine pointer only) offers a document-comment menu item that stores an unanchored `anchorType: "document"` comment; its card reads document-wide with no jump button, Copy all describes the document-wide anchor, right-clicking a link or other interactive/selectable target leaves the native menu, and the menu mode resets to text afterward. | `tests/42-doc-comment.spec.js` - full document-comment suite; source: `assets/js/` |
| CMH-DOCCMT-02 | A real desktop right-click (mousedown, mouseup, then contextmenu) on empty space keeps the document-comment menu open: neither a right-button `mouseup` (button 2) nor a macOS Ctrl-click gesture (a primary-button `mouseup` with `ctrlKey` held) runs the text-selection cleanup that would queue a `hideMenu()` and make the menu flicker open then vanish. | `tests/42-doc-comment.spec.js` - `a real right-click (mousedown, mouseup, then contextmenu) keeps the doc-comment menu open`, `a macOS Ctrl-click (mouseup button:0 ctrlKey, then contextmenu) keeps the doc-comment menu open`; source: `assets/js/` |

## Layered checklist

A `data-cmh-checklist` container is a hierarchical checklist. Any descendant carrying `data-cmh-state` or `data-cmh-item` is an item; an item with child items (nested lists, or table rows that name a parent via `data-cmh-parent`) is a branch that aggregates over its DIRECT children, otherwise a leaf. Leaf state cycles `blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank`; a branch click propagates its next state to every descendant leaf. Only leaves whose state differs from their authored `data-cmh-state` baseline are stored, as one-character codes under `COMMENT_KEY + "::cl"`. Changes surface as one per-list card (jump + reset) placed by document order, a Copy-all section with `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON`, and an export bake into `data-cmh-state`. Item identity is `data-cmh-item` when present, else the 1-based document-order position within the checklist; identity is never rendered (no visible numeration), so a checklist can live in a sortable table.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-CHECK-01 | An authored checklist renders one injected `cm-skip` state control per item; the container gets `cmh-checklist-ready`, and each item is tagged `data-cmh-check-role="leaf"` or `"branch"` (a branch is an item with child items). | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-01: authored checklist renders controls; leaf vs branch detected` |
| CMH-CHECK-02 | Clicking a leaf control cycles its state `blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank`. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-02: a leaf cycles blank -> check -> cross -> question -> blank` |
| CMH-CHECK-03 | A branch's state derives from its direct children: all-same shows that state, any disagreement shows `mixed`; authored `data-cmh-state` on a branch is ignored. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-03: a branch aggregates over its direct children (all-same, else mixed)` |
| CMH-CHECK-04 | Clicking a branch cycles it to its next state (from `mixed`, to `check`) and sets every descendant leaf to that state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-04: clicking a branch propagates its next state to all descendant leaves` |
| CMH-CHECK-05 | Only leaves whose state differs from the authored baseline are stored (minimal one-character-code deltas under `::cl`); cycling a leaf back to baseline prunes it, and states restore on reload. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-05: only changed leaves persist (minimal delta), restored on reload` |
| CMH-CHECK-06 | Each checklist with changes renders one non-comment card with a Reset button that reverts that checklist to its authored baseline; the comment count stays 0. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-06: one card per changed list; Reset reverts that list to baseline` |
| CMH-CHECK-07 | A checklist change card is placed by document order among the comment cards (not pinned on top), using the checklist container's text position. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-07: the checklist card is placed by document order among comments` |
| CMH-CHECK-08 | Copy all emits a `## Checklist "<id>"` section listing each changed item's `from -> to`, plus a machine-readable `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON: {...}` line for the cementing tool. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-08: Copy all includes a checklist section and CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON` |
| CMH-CHECK-09 | Exporting bakes each leaf's current state into its `data-cmh-state` attribute so the exported file reflects the new states. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-09: export bakes current states into data-cmh-state` |
| CMH-CHECK-10 | A table-shape checklist builds its tree from `data-cmh-parent` references (rows cannot nest), and aggregation and top-down propagation work across those links. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-10: table-shape parent links build the tree; aggregation and propagation work` |
| CMH-CHECK-11 | An item's label stays ordinary commentable content (a text comment on it creates a highlight); only the injected icon control carries `cm-skip`. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-11: item labels stay text-commentable; the control is cm-skip` |
| CMH-CHECK-12 | The control is keyboard-operable (Enter/Space cycle it) and its `aria-label` announces the item label and current state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-12: keyboard cycles the control and ARIA announces the state` |
| CMH-CHECK-13 | `tools/checklist/checklist_apply.py` reads a `CHECKLIST_STATE_JSON` map (from a bundle or `--state-json`) and rewrites `data-cmh-state` on each named item (by `data-cmh-item` id or positional key) deterministically and idempotently, inserting the attribute when absent and skipping invalid tokens. | `tests/test_checklist_apply.py` - `ChecklistApplyTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-14 | `tools/checklist/checklist_scaffold.py` turns an indented outline into validator-clean list or table markup with stable slug ids, `data-cmh-parent` links for the table shape, per-item baseline states, and no `data-cmh-state` on branches. | `tests/test_checklist_scaffold.py` - `ChecklistScaffoldTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-15 | `validate.py` flags checklist authoring mistakes as strict-escalated warnings (duplicate container ids, invalid state tokens, an empty checklist, duplicate item ids, unresolved `data-cmh-parent`), and is a no-op for a checklist-free document. | `tests/test_validate_checklist.py` - `ChecklistValidateTests` |
| CMH-CHECK-BADGE | An unsaved checklist state change flips the document badge to Not portable with a reason naming the checklist, until the file is re-exported. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-BADGE: an unsaved checklist change flips the badge to Not portable` |
| CMH-CHECK-16 | A table-row item's label is taken from its first content cell (the cell after the state cell), not a concatenation of every remaining cell, so extra columns like an Owner do not leak into the change record or the Copy-all bundle. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-16: a table item's label is its first content cell, not every other cell` |
| CMH-CHECK-17 | A document that loads already having a persisted checklist change opens the comments sidebar at startup (not only on the live 0 -> >0 change transition), so the per-list change card is visible instead of hidden in a closed panel. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-17: a checklist that loads with a persisted change opens the sidebar so the card is seen` |
| CMH-CHECK-18 | Clear restores every changed checklist item to its authored `data-cmh-state` baseline, removes the checklist change card, and prunes the persisted `::cl` state. | `tests/59-checklist.spec.js` - `CMH-CHECK-18: Clear restores checklist state changes to the authored baseline`; source: `assets/js/` - `resetAllChecklists()`, Clear handler |

## Editable notes fields

A `data-cmh-note="<id>"` element is a single editable free-text field. The runtime replaces its
authored text with a `<textarea>` (with a single/multi-line toggle) whose baseline is the authored,
canonically-normalized `textContent`. Only a note whose current text differs from baseline is stored,
as `{id:text}` under `COMMENT_KEY + "::note"`; edits surface as one per-note change card (jump + reset)
placed by document order, a Copy-all section with `NOTES_STATE_JSON`, and an export bake into the
element's text. The field is `cm-skip` (editing never creates a highlight) and is set up before offset
restoration so its excluded text does not shift comment offsets. The one canonical normalizer (LF line
endings, trimmed outer whitespace) is implemented identically in the runtime and in
`tools/notes/notes_apply.py` so the browser and the cementing tool agree.

| Feature | Behavior | Test |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-NOTE-01 | An authored `data-cmh-note` element upgrades to an editable `<textarea>`; the container gets `cm-skip` and `cmh-note-ready`, seeded with the authored text, alongside a label chip and a single/multi-line toggle. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-01: authored note upgrades to an editable textarea (cm-skip, ready, toggle)` |
| CMH-NOTE-02 | An edit stores a minimal delta (only when the normalized text differs from baseline) under `::note`; it restores on reload, and editing back to the exact baseline prunes the entry. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-02: an edit persists a minimal delta, restores on reload, and prunes at baseline` |
| CMH-NOTE-03 | A per-note toggle switches the field between single-line and multi-line (row count). | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-03: the single/multi-line toggle switches the field height` |
| CMH-NOTE-04 | Each changed note renders one non-comment card, placed by document order among the comment cards; the comment count stays 0. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-04: a changed note renders one non-comment card by document order; count stays 0` |
| CMH-NOTE-05 | The card's reset reverts the field to its authored baseline and prunes the delta. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-05: reset reverts the note to its authored baseline` |
| CMH-NOTE-06 | The global Clear all comments also reverts every changed note to its authored baseline and prunes the `::note` state. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-06: Clear all comments also reverts note edits to baseline` |
| CMH-NOTE-07 | An unsaved note edit flips the document badge to Not portable with a reason naming the note, until the file is re-exported. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-07: an unsaved note edit flips the badge to Not portable, naming the note` |
| CMH-NOTE-08 | Copy all emits a `## Note "<id>"` section with the from/to text plus a machine-readable `NOTES_STATE_JSON: {...}` line. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-08: Copy all includes a Note section and NOTES_STATE_JSON` |
| CMH-NOTE-09 | Every export bakes the current note text into the element (via `textContent`, never `innerHTML`) with no runtime editing attributes, so the exported source carries the edit safely. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-09: export bakes note text into the source element with no editing attributes` |
| CMH-NOTE-10 | A changed note is searchable: its change card filters by the note label and text like a comment card (rather than being hidden while a query is active). | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-10: a changed note is searchable in the sidebar` |
| CMH-NOTE-11 | A document that loads already having a persisted note edit opens the comments sidebar at startup so the change card is visible. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-11: a note that loads with a persisted edit opens the sidebar` |
| CMH-NOTE-12 | The note field is `cm-skip`: selecting or typing in it never creates a highlight comment. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-12: the note field is cm-skip; editing it never creates a highlight` |
| CMH-NOTE-E2E | The full reviewer-edit round-trip: an in-browser edit produces a Copy-all bundle whose `NOTES_STATE_JSON` `tools/notes/notes_apply.py` cements into the source file, and reopening the cemented file (keeping the stale storage override) shows the baked text, no pending change, and a Portable badge. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-E2E: reviewer edit -> Copy all -> notes_apply.py -> reopen shows the cemented source` |
| CMH-NOTE-13 | `tools/notes/notes_apply.py` cements each `NOTES_STATE_JSON` value into its `data-cmh-note` element's inner content (HTML-escaped, newline-style preserved) deterministically and idempotently, from a bundle or `--state-json`, filling an empty element and skipping unknown ids. | `tests/test_notes_apply.py` - `NotesApplyTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-14 | `tools/notes/notes_scaffold.py` emits validator-clean `data-cmh-note` markup with a stable id, an optional label, escaped text, an optional multi-line default, and an optional foldable flag. | `tests/test_notes_scaffold.py` - `NotesScaffoldTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-15 | `validate.py` flags notes authoring mistakes as strict-escalated warnings (duplicate id, empty id, a nested note, a void-element note, a note with child elements, a note inside a checklist/diff/widget/deck substrate), and is a no-op for a notes-free document. | `tests/test_notes_validate.py` - `NotesValidateTests` |
| CMH-NOTE-16 | A `data-cmh-note-foldable` note is a disclosure: a `+`/`-` toggle on the header line reveals or hides the field on the line below. It starts collapsed only when empty (a note with content, authored or a persisted edit, starts expanded); the fold state is session-only, evaluated once at load, so a manual collapse sticks. The toggle is an accessible `<button>` (`aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`), a collapsed note that still holds content is badged, `jumpToNote` expands a collapsed note before focusing it, and the collapse never reaches storage or the exported source. | `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-16: a foldable note starts collapsed only when empty and toggles with the +/- control`, `CMH-NOTE-16: keyboard toggles the fold, and a collapsed note with content shows a badge`, `CMH-NOTE-16: a foldable note with a persisted edit auto-expands on reload; jump expands a collapsed note`, `CMH-NOTE-16: fold state is presentation only - the export keeps data-cmh-note-foldable but no runtime collapse` |

## Export, embedded comments, and output modes

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-EXP-01 | `Export as Portable` downloads `<stem>-portable.html`, strips any existing `-comments` or `-portable` suffix before adding it, and writes current comments into `<script id="embeddedComments">` with `<` escaped as `\u003c`. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - closing-script escaping and embeds comment; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `Export as Portable does not stack suffixes on a *-comments.html file`; source: `assets/js/` - `_suggestedFilename()` |
| CMH-EXP-02 | A downloaded copy with embedded comments opens in a fresh browser and shows those comments without relying on existing `localStorage`. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `embedded comments travel: a shared copy shows them in a fresh browser (no localStorage)`; `tests/34-modes.spec.js` - `MODE Portable-with-comments: Save embeds comments that travel to a fresh browser and stay Portable` |
| CMH-EXP-03 | On load, embedded comments merge into localStorage by id; the item with the later `updatedAt`, or fallback `createdAt`, wins, and disjoint ids are preserved. | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `embedded comments merge by latest updatedAt (both directions)`; source: `assets/js/` - `mergeCommentSets()`, `loadComments()` |
| CMH-EXP-04 | `Export to Plain HTML` removes handled ids, embedded comments, the real `commentableHtmlLayer` descriptor, comment UI, and runtime JS while preserving host content, shipped content styling, localStorage, and host scripts that only carry a decoy `data-id="commentableHtmlLayer"` attribute. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - plain strips layer but keeps content; `tests/12-features.spec.js` - unwraps highlights, drops `sidebar-open`, leaves localStorage; `tests/10-more.spec.js` - forged marker no leak, `Export plain ignores a data-id commentableHtmlLayer decoy script` |
| CMH-EXP-05 | In nonportable mode, Plain HTML keeps the CSS companion link for styling and removes only the runtime companion and comment DOM. | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `Export plain in nonportable mode strips the JS companion but keeps the CSS companion and content` |
| CMH-EXP-06 | On `file://` (where `fetch()` of the page is blocked) every export falls back to a DOM snapshot as its base. The snapshot is captured before any runtime mutation but is completed at export time with the host content parsed after the layer `<script>` (per charts-embedding.md, chart data and init scripts placed after the `END: commentable-html - JS` marker), so a chart or other tail content is never dropped from a file:// Portable or Plain export. The recovered tail excludes layer-injected chrome (footer, side-TOC, scroll progress, tooltips - all `cm-skip`) so that chrome never leaks into an export. | `tests/45-file-export-tail.spec.js` - Portable and Plain each keep a chart placed after the JS marker; `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js`, `tests/22-footer-show.spec.js` - the side-TOC/Help/footer never leak into a Plain or embedded-comment export; source: `assets/js/` - `_snapshotWithTail()`, `CMH_LAYER_SCRIPT` |
| CMH-EXP-07 | The embedded-comments block is located by a real, whitespace-delimited `id="embeddedComments"` attribute regardless of the remaining attribute order or spacing, so a document authored or re-saved as `<script id="embeddedComments" type="application/json">` still receives the current comments on export. A decoy `data-id="embeddedComments"` or `aria-id="embeddedComments"` on another script is never mistaken for the real block, so the export cannot update the wrong script and leave real comments stale. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `embedded-comments script is found regardless of attribute order (CMH-EXP-07)`, `export updates the real embeddedComments block, not a decoy data-id script (CMH-EXP-07)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-08 | Save, Portable, and Offline exports filter the canonical export comment set through `handledCommentIds`, matching Copy all: a comment already marked handled never reappears in embedded export JSON even if it is still present in live memory. | `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - `Save, Portable, and Offline exports exclude comments already listed as handled`; source: `assets/js/` - `_exportableComments()` |
| CMH-EXP-09 | Every export path (Save, Portable, Offline, and Plain) normalizes the base HTML by stripping the transient runtime body-state classes (`sidebar-open`, `cm-sidebar-resizing`, `cm-widget-dragging`) from the `<body>` open tag, so a stale or open-sidebar source never bakes that state into a saved or exported file (a persisted `sidebar-open` would render the content full width with an empty sidebar gutter via the `body.sidebar-open .app` layout rule). The normalizer touches only the FIRST `<body>` open tag (a `<body class=...>` literal inside a later script/content is left alone), handles double-quoted, single-quoted, and unquoted class values, matches whole class tokens (a superstring like `x-sidebar-open` is preserved), and removes an emptied class attribute. Non-transient body classes are preserved; the live layer re-derives the sidebar state on load. | `tests/54-export-body-normalize.spec.js` - `Save/Portable export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Offline export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Plain export drops sidebar-open and other transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, `Portable export from a nonportable file (snapshot fallback) drops transient body classes (CMH-EXP-09)`, and the `Body-class normalizer unit (CMH-EXP-09)` suite (`single-quoted transient class is stripped to the kept classes`, `a <body class> literal after the real body (in a script) is not mutated`, `a superstring host class like x-sidebar-open is preserved`, `the existing double-quoted case still strips only the transient token`, `an emptied class attribute is removed and unquoted values are handled`); source: `assets/js/` - `_stripTransientBodyClasses()`, `_getBaseHtml()`, `_buildPlainHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-10 | `Export as Portable` preserves a comment note verbatim even when it contains a `$`-replacement pattern (`$&`, `$1`, `$$`, and the dollar-backtick and dollar-quote forms): the saved-HTML builder splices the embedded-comments region with a function replacer, so `String.replace` never expands those patterns from the note text and the stored JSON round-trips exactly on reload. | `tests/55-export-dollar-and-markers.spec.js` - `Export as Portable preserves a comment note containing $-replacement patterns (F2)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-11 | `Export to Plain HTML` strips a comment-data region whose banner uses the compact single-line marker form (no `===` decoration) that the validator's `=*` marker grammar also accepts, so a compact-markered document exports to plain cleanly without tripping the leak-guard safety net; the leak guard itself matches `id="handledCommentIds"`/`id="embeddedComments"` regardless of quote style or surrounding whitespace. | `tests/55-export-dollar-and-markers.spec.js` - `Export as Plain strips a compact-markered EMBEDDED COMMENTS region (F3)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildPlainHtml()` |
| CMH-EXP-12 | The export path (a proven bug hot spot) is guarded by a property/fuzz test and a cross-implementation grammar test: a batch of adversarial comment notes (every `$`-replacement pattern, escaped `<`, `<!--`, `</script`, unicode, backticks, and literal region-marker phrases) each round-trips byte-exact through Export as Portable and `JSON.parse` with no expansion, escaping loss, truncation, or forged region boundary; and Export to Plain strips compact-markered comment-data regions across all of HANDLED IDS, EMBEDDED COMMENTS, and COMMENT UI (not just one), matching the validator's `=*` grammar. | `tests/56-export-fuzz.spec.js` - `Export as Portable round-trips a batch of adversarial comment notes byte-exact (GH-REGRESS-EXPORT-HOTSPOT)`, `Export as Plain strips compact-markered comment-data regions across all of them (GH-REGRESS-EXPORT-HOTSPOT)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_buildSavedHtml()`, `_buildPlainHtml()` |

| CMH-MODE-05 | The nonportable version handshake compares the document meta version, assets registry version, and runtime `CMH_VERSION` with semver compatibility: a same-major runtime that is newer than or equal to the page is compatible and shows no banner, a same-major runtime older than the page shows a soft update banner, and a different major shows an incompatible-runtime banner. Malformed versions fall back to raw-string mismatch warnings without crashing. | `tests/07-nonportable.spec.js` - older same-major no banner, different-major incompatible banner, newer same-major soft banner; source: `assets/js/` - `parseSemver()`, `runtimeCompatibleWith()`, `versionBannerMessage()` |

| CMH-DOC-08 | The `SKILL.md` discovery description and the `plugin.json` marketplace description are intentionally different surfaces (agent trigger vs human blurb) but share a whole-word core vocabulary (`html`, `comment`, a review-family word, and `portable`), so the two cannot silently drift into telling different stories. | `tests/test_docs_diagrams.py` - `DescriptionConsistencyDocsTests`; source: `SKILL.md`, `plugin.json` |

## Export to Markdown

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-MD-01 | `Export to Markdown` deterministically converts `#commentRoot` content to GFM (headings, tables, fenced code with language, mermaid source not SVG, lists, blockquotes, image figures, KQL kusto fences with the run link), appends live comments as a `Review comments` section, and downloads a `.md` file (download only, never writing the clipboard), and is reachable from the sidebar and the overflow menu. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - kitchen-sink structure, determinism, callout/chart/diff forms, mermaid source, comment appendix, `Export to Markdown downloads a .md file and does NOT write the clipboard`, sidebar/overflow availability; source: `assets/js/` - `htmlToMarkdown()`, `buildMarkdownDoc()`, `exportMarkdown()` |
| CMH-MD-02 | Markdown export is injection-safe: prose, comment notes, and attribute-derived labels cannot forge headings, blockquotes, lists, thematic rules, setext underlines, GFM tables, or raw HTML; link/image labels and URLs are escaped; `javascript:`/control-char URLs are neutralized; code spans and fences stay intact against backtick and pipe tricks. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - the forge/escape/neutralize suite (setext, table, backslash destination, control-char scheme, pipe-in-cell, fence voiding, HTML injection); source: `assets/js/` - `_mdText()`, `_mdEscapeLeading()`, `_mdEscapePipes()`, `_mdUrl()` |
| CMH-MD-03 | List items serialize inline `<strong>`/`<em>`/`<a>`/`<code>`/`<img>` that are DIRECT children of the `<li>` (not only inside a nested block), so a bullet mixing `**bold**`, an inline `<a>` link, and `code` keeps its emphasis, link, and code span in the exported Markdown. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `Markdown export serializes strong/link/code that are direct list-item children (CMH-MD-03)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdInlineOne()`, `_mdAppendInline()`, `_mdList()` |
| CMH-MD-04 | The Markdown URL allowlist keeps only image data URLs: a bare `data:` destination (for example `data:text/html`) is neutralized to `about:blank`, while a `data:image/...` source is preserved so inline images still export. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `Markdown export drops a bare data: URL but keeps a data:image URL (CMH-MD-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdUrl()` |
| CMH-MD-05 | Markdown export serializes `data-cm-widget` content even when the widget is `cm-skip`: slotted boards export as a `_[Widget: ...]_` note plus a GFM table whose columns are `data-cm-slot` values and whose cells are `data-cm-part-label` values; unslotted widgets export as a bullet list. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `expanded demo item kinds convert to Markdown`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdWidget()` |
| CMH-MD-06 | Markdown export deliberately omits `data-cm-offline-chart` image payloads to avoid base64 bloat, emitting only the offline chart snapshot's caption or alt text as a chart-snapshot placeholder. | `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `offline chart snapshots export label text without embedding the data image (CMH-MD-06)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_mdFigure()` |

| CMH-SIDE-06 | The comments sidebar has a keyboard-focusable `cm-skip` resize separator on its inner edge. Dragging or using Left/Right/Home/End changes `--cm-sidebar-w`, persists the width in localStorage, clamps to viewport min/max values, updates reserved page space, and switches header buttons to wrapped rows when narrow. The drag/keyboard minimum is 256px on both wide and narrow screens - the empirically measured floor at which the two-per-row export button labels (`Portable`, `Offline`, `Markdown`, `Plain HTML`), Copy all, and the search placeholder stay fully legible (below ~240px they clip); the panel's CSS `min-width` floor matches (`min(16rem, calc(100vw - 24px))`) so the pane never renders narrower, and the floor is still clamped to the viewport on very small screens. | `tests/21-sidebar-polish.spec.js` - `the sidebar minimum width keeps every action button label legible (CMH-SIDE-06)`, resize persistence, reserved layout, and narrow header wrapping tests; source: `assets/js/` - `setupSidebarResize()`, `_sidebarWidthBounds()`; `assets/css/` - `.cm-sidebar` min-width |

| CMH-SEARCH-01 | The comments panel has a single search field (with a leading magnifier icon) that filters the rendered comment cards to only those whose reviewer note text matches the query case-insensitively as a substring (the quoted anchor content, section path, and pin are NOT searched - see CMH-SEARCH-04). A shown/total count (for example `2 / 3`) sits beside the field, and a clear (X) button appears while the field is non-empty; clicking it empties the field, restores every card, and refocuses the field. Chrome (action-button labels, the meta line) is never matched, and non-comment cards (widget layout-change and checklist cards) are hidden while a search is active. The filter is re-applied after every render, so it survives adding, editing, or sorting comments. | `tests/60-comment-search.spec.js` - `filters the comment list case-insensitively with a shown/total count and a clear button (CMH-SEARCH-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `applyCommentSearch()`, `setupCommentSearch()`; `assets/template.shell.html`; `assets/css/` |

Content is grouped into collapsible topics with a live search box (focused on open) that filters topics and entries and shows an empty-state when nothing matches. In the sidebar header, Help and Hide are a right-aligned grouped pair (small gap, visually distinct Hide) and Hide carries a right-facing collapse chevron. | `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - Help open, Escape close, focus restore, Tab trap tests; `tests/31-ui-batch4.spec.js` - `Help and Hide are a grouped pair with a small gap, and Hide has a right chevron`; `tests/32-ui-batch5.spec.js` - `Help has many collapsible topics, a search box that gets focus`, `typing in the search box filters topics`, `a collapsed topic expands when its summary is clicked`; `tests/37-ui-polish.spec.js` - `the help buttons are labelled Help & About (CMH-HELP-LABEL-01)`, `help opens with the 4-step review workflow in the Getting started topic (CMH-WF-HELP-01)` |

The filter box hides when the menu is collapsed and clears when the viewport narrows below the menu breakpoint. Filtered sections use `display:none` (offset-safe), and scroll-spy never marks a filtered entry `aria-current` - including a flat heading with no `<section>` wrapper (whose menu row still filters). | `tests/65-toc-search.spec.js` - `the search box filters visible sections by heading and body text, Escape clears (CMH-TOC-09)`, `navigating to a filtered-out section reveals it (CMH-TOC-09)`, `the filter box hides when the side menu is collapsed (CMH-TOC-09)`, `jumping to a comment inside a filtered-out section reveals it (CMH-TOC-09)`, `a filtered-out flat heading entry never receives aria-current (CMH-TOC-09)`; source: `assets/js/` - `setupSideToc()` `applyTocFilter()` `onScroll()`, `50-sidebar.js` `expandCollapsedAncestors()`; `assets/css/` - `.cm-side-toc-search`, `section.cm-toc-filtered` |

## Content, visual conventions, KQL, charts, and optional loaders

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-THEME-01 | The template defaults to light theme; `?clawpilotTheme=dark` forces dark; `?clawpilotTheme=auto` follows OS preference. | `tests/01-inline.spec.js` - `renders in a light theme by default`; `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `renders and works in dark theme (clawpilotTheme=dark)`; `tests/33-coverage-gaps.spec.js` - `clawpilotTheme=auto follows the OS color scheme (CMH-THEME-01)`; `tests/23-coverage.spec.js` - `CMH-THEME-01: template defaults to light, supports dark, and follows OS for auto` |
| CMH-THEME-02 | The flat-doc validator evaluates authored `--cp-*` theme overrides for WCAG readability, resolving the light (`:root`) and dark (`[data-theme="dark"]`) environments SEPARATELY (a token overridden only in dark is judged against its dark value, matching custom-property cascade). It runs only on tokens the author changed from the shipped defaults, so the accepted defaults are never flagged. Graduated severity: text/link pairs use a 4.5:1 target - a near-miss in the 3.0-4.49 band is an advisory WARNING, below 3.0:1 is an ERROR; non-text UI pairs use a 3.0:1 bar (below it is an ERROR). A chain that cannot be resolved to two concrete colors is reported as an 'unresolved / not evaluated' advisory. This is a STATIC resolution, NOT a computed-style/cascade simulation (documented non-goal). `--suggest` prints a compliant nudged foreground for a finding when one is reachable (unresolved and mid-tone-unreachable findings get a diagnostic instead). A `<style data-cmh-brand>` palette is deferred to the `--brand` tooling's own contrast advisory (CMH-TOOL-19) and not double-flagged here. The near-miss and unresolved advisories carry a stable prefix so `retrofit.py` keeps them out of its hard-fail path; a bad-contrast ERROR still blocks retrofit. | `tests/test_theme_contrast.py` - `test_cmh_theme_02_environments_resolve_dark_overrides_separately`, `test_cmh_theme_02_nudge_reaches_target`, `test_cmh_theme_02_nudge_returns_none_when_unreachable`, `test_cmh_theme_02_grouped_selector_applies_to_both_envs`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_media_block_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_theme_02_screen_conditional_groups_are_traversed`, `test_cmh_theme_02_statement_at_rules_do_not_swallow_the_next_rule`, `test_cmh_theme_02_brace_in_declaration_value_does_not_drop_the_rule`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_style_block_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_theme_02_brand_stamped_palette_is_deferred`, `test_cmh_theme_02_not_screen_media_is_excluded`, `test_cmh_theme_02_compact_at_rule_syntax_is_traversed`, `test_cmh_theme_02_deeply_nested_groups_do_not_crash`, `test_cmh_theme_02_compliant_palette_is_clean`, `test_cmh_theme_02_bad_text_contrast_is_an_error`, `test_cmh_theme_02_near_miss_is_a_warning_not_an_error`, `test_cmh_theme_02_dark_only_override_is_caught_in_dark_env_only`, `test_cmh_theme_02_print_block_does_not_mask_bad_screen_dark_override`, `test_cmh_theme_02_bad_override_in_screen_media_is_evaluated`, `test_cmh_theme_02_partial_override_pairs_against_default`, `test_cmh_theme_02_undefined_tokens_are_not_fabricated`, `test_cmh_theme_02_reformatted_default_is_not_an_override`, `test_cmh_theme_02_unresolved_chain_is_an_advisory`, `test_cmh_theme_02_unresolved_background_is_an_advisory`, `test_cmh_theme_02_non_text_ui_uses_three_to_one`, `test_cmh_theme_02_findings_carry_a_reachable_suggestion`, `test_cmh_theme_02_advisory_is_not_fatal_for_retrofit`, `test_cmh_theme_02_defaults_match_shipped_template`; source: `tools/validate/cmhval/contrast.py` (`theme_environments`, `nudge_to_ratio`), `tools/validate/checks/theme_contrast.py`, wired in `tools/validate/validate.py` |
| CMH-CONTENT-01 | Top-level sections inside `#commentRoot` render as cards with border, radius, padding, and shadow; nested sections are not double-carded. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - section card, nested scoping, and dark theme tests |
| CMH-CONTENT-02 | Plain tables inside `#commentRoot` get collapsed borders, themed headers, and zebra striping. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `tables use collapsed borders and a themed header` |
| CMH-CONTENT-03 | `.badge`, `.badge.ok`, `.badge.warn`, and `.badge.danger` render as status pills with accessible themed contrast; long badge text wraps inside the pill and never overflows (no `white-space: nowrap`; `max-width: 100%`; `overflow-wrap: anywhere`). | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - badge style and dark theme tests; `tests/29-overflow.spec.js` - `a long .badge wraps inside its box instead of overflowing` |
| CMH-CONTENT-04 | Author `nav.cm-toc` markup is styled and its anchor links resolve to stable section ids. | `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` - `a table of contents renders with anchor links that resolve to sections` |
| CMH-CONTENT-05 | ADO repos, files, PRs, work items, builds, and wiki references in authored content should be real links to the source resource. | manual - documented in `SKILL.md`; no automated enforcement found |
| CMH-CONTENT-06 | Cross-references between sections should use stable in-page anchor links instead of prose such as `see above`. | partial - `tests/16-formatting.spec.js` verifies TOC anchor links; manual for prose cross-reference coverage |
| CMH-CONTENT-07 | Rectangular authored tables with a `<thead>` are sortable per column with up/down chevrons; only tables without `colspan`/`rowspan` and with matching cell counts opt in, and each click cycles asc, desc, original. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `every table column header gets sort chevrons`, `a numeric column sorts numerically, cycling asc -> desc -> original`, `a text column sorts lexicographically`; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `a non-rectangular table (colspan) is not made sortable`; source: `assets/js/` - `_sortableTables()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-08 | Table sorting is numeric-aware with empty cells first and no `NaN` comparisons; sort state persists to `localStorage`, re-applies before comment restore, recomputes live text offsets after sort, and canonicalizes comments back to original row order for export. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `sorting keeps comment anchors attached, and the sort survives reload`; `tests/28-duck-fixes.spec.js` - `sorting a numeric column with multiple empty cells does not scramble (NaN guard)`, `a comment on a sorted table cell exports with a correct anchor`; source: `assets/js/` - `_sortRows()`, `_canonicalCommentsForExport()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-09 | The shipped content layout boxes standalone code blocks and `figure.chart`, widens the template content max width to 1480px, and keeps code/chart figures readable without host CSS. | `tests/26-ui-batch2.spec.js` - `standalone code blocks are boxed`; `tests/27-ui-batch3.spec.js` - `chart figures are boxed`; source: `assets/template.shell.html` - `.app { max-width: 1480px; }` |
| CMH-CONTENT-10 | Author text never runs out of its box: long badges, long table cells, and long KQL cluster titles wrap within their container instead of overflowing or being clipped (`overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `max-width: 100%`, and `min-width: 0` on the flex KQL title). | `tests/29-overflow.spec.js` - long badge, long table cell, and long KQL title all stay in-box; `tests/30-monkey.spec.js` - random-interaction fuzz never throws |
| CMH-CONTENT-11 | Boxed asides use theme-aware `cmh-callout` (plus `-info`, `-success`, `-warning`, `-danger`) utilities and a boxed `cmh-lede` lead; all keep readable text (WCAG AA contrast) on soft tinted backgrounds in both light and dark themes, so authors never hardcode colors. | `tests/36-callouts.spec.js` - `callouts and lede stay readable in light theme (CMH-CALLOUT-01)`, `... dark theme`, `every callout variant renders as a bordered box`, `callout prose is commentable (CMH-CALLOUT-02)`; docs: `../pkg/skills/commentable-html/references/content-conventions.md` |
| CMH-CALLOUT-03 | Callouts carry a NON-color affordance so meaning survives grayscale printing, color-blindness, and screen readers: each variant renders a distinct leading `::before` glyph (info/success/warning/danger) drawn in `--cp-text` with text (not emoji) presentation and hidden from the accessibility tree via the `content: ".." / ""` alt-text syntax, and a runtime pass stamps `role="note"` plus a variant `aria-label` (`Note`/`Success`/`Warning`/`Danger`). An explicit author `aria-label` is respected, and a leading `<strong>` label (the first meaningful node, robust to a stray empty leading element, but not mid-sentence bold) suppresses the auto aria-label so the variant is not announced twice. The glyph is a pseudo-element and the placement uses logical properties (RTL-safe), so it never enters text selection or comment offsets. | `tests/66-callout-a11y.spec.js` - `each callout variant exposes role=note and the correct variant accessible name (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an explicit author aria-label is respected (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `each callout variant renders a distinct non-color glyph (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an authored leading strong label suppresses the aria-label (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `mid-sentence bold does NOT suppress the aria-label (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`, `an empty leading element does not defeat strong-label suppression (CMH-CALLOUT-03)`; source: `assets/js/39-callout.js`; `assets/css/` - `.cmh-callout::before` |
| CMH-CONTENT-12 | Each commentable code block shows a small language pill (Python, C#, KQL, and so on) derived from its `language-*` class, sitting next to the Copy button in a shared tools container. | `tests/37-ui-polish.spec.js` - `code blocks show a language pill beside the Copy button (CMH-CODEPILL-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `setupCodeCopy()`, `_codeLangLabel()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-14 | Top-level prose is not width-capped: ordinary `#commentRoot` paragraphs (and the `cmh-lede` lead) fill the content column, the same full width as tables, figures, code, and callouts (there is no readable-measure cap). | `tests/46-heading-a11y-title.spec.js` - `top-level prose paragraphs are not width-capped (lede and sections both full width)`, `tests/44-ui-parity.spec.js` - `top-level prose paragraphs fill the content width (no measure cap)`; source: `assets/css/` |
| CMH-CONTENT-15 | A document has a themed, visible top-level title: `#commentRoot` top-level `h1` (bare or inside the lede header) is styled larger than body prose, and `new_document.py` prepends a lede header carrying the label as an `<h1>` when the fragment has none (`--no-title` opts out; a fragment with its own `h1`/lede is left untouched). The "already has a title" check parses the fragment (it does not scan raw text), so an `<h1>` inside an HTML comment, `<script>`, or `<style>`, or an `h1`/`cmh-lede` nested below the top level, does not suppress the prepended title. | `tests/46-heading-a11y-title.spec.js` - `the document title h1 is themed and visibly larger than body prose`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `DocTitleTests` incl. `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_h1_inside_comment`, `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_h1_inside_script`, `test_ensure_doc_title_ignores_nested_h1_and_lede`; source: `assets/css/` - `#commentRoot > h1`, `#commentRoot > header > h1`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py` - `ensure_doc_title()`, `_has_active_title()` |
| CMH-CONTENT-16 | The reviewable content region is exactly the `#commentRoot` subtree: `generate_toc.py` and `validate.py` both track the root by identity and mark it closed on the root's (or an ancestor's) end tag, so headings and prose cross-references in a later sibling container (for example a page `<footer>` after `</main id=commentRoot>`) are not collected into the TOC nor validated as content, even though they sit at the same nesting depth. | `tests/test_generate_toc.py` - `test_headings_after_root_close_are_ignored`; `tests/test_validate_references.py` - `test_reference_after_root_close_ignored`; source: `tools/authoring/generate_toc.py` - `_inside_root()`, `root_closed`; `tools/validate/checks/parsing.py` - `_cr_closed` |
| CMH-STATS-01 | A report/plan opens with a `cm-skip` document-overview strip (`div.cmh-doc-stats[data-cmh-doc-stats]`) placed directly under the `<h1>` title, reporting the section count (`<h2>` count), word count, and approximate reading time (words / 200 wpm, rounded up, floor of one minute). `tools/authoring/doc_stats.py` computes and injects it: idempotent (refreshes stale counts in place), excluded from its own word count (it is `cm-skip` and the counter also skips `nav.cm-toc`/`script`/`style`/`template`), and baked into the content so it survives Plain/Standalone export. `new_document.py`, `finalize.py`, and `retrofit.py` bake it by DEFAULT for report/plan; opt out with `--no-stats`. | `tests/test_doc_stats.py` - all count, reading-time, placement, idempotence, refresh, cm-skip-exclusion, and CLI tests; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_bakes_doc_stats_for_report_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`, `test_stats_skipped_for_non_card_kind`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_report_gets_doc_stats_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`; `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_report_gets_doc_stats_by_default`, `test_no_stats_flag_skips_the_overview_strip`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stats.py`, `assets/css/50-content.css` |
| CMH-CONTENT-13 | Content `<pre>` and `<pre><code>` blocks use `white-space: pre-wrap` so long lines wrap within their container rather than overflowing horizontally; diff (`pre.cmh-diff`) and mermaid (`pre.mermaid`) blocks use their own whitespace handling and are unaffected. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `content pre blocks wrap while diff and mermaid pre blocks keep their own whitespace (CMH-CONTENT-13)`; source: `assets/css/` |
| CMH-HL-01 | The runtime highlights a commentable `<pre><code class="language-XXX">` block that shipped without highlight spans, on load, using the in-page tokenizer (the same `cmh-code-*` classes as the author-time highlighter), so a labelled block never renders as plain monochrome text even when the author never baked highlighting. It runs before the line-number gutter and before comment restoration, so line numbers and text-offset anchoring stay consistent (a comment on a now-highlighted block round-trips through reload). It is idempotent (an already-highlighted block is left alone) and only fires for a language the tokenizer knows (`diffLangKnown`); the known set includes the markup family, so a raw `language-html`/`language-xml` block also self-heals (tag names, attribute-value strings, and `<!-- -->` comments), while an unknown/non-tokenizable label (`language-text`, `language-kusto`) stays plain. | `tests/61-code-highlight-fallback.spec.js` - `a language-labelled block with no spans is highlighted on load; unknown labels stay plain`, `an unbaked language-html and language-xml block is highlighted on load (markup family) (CMH-HL-01)`, `an already-highlighted (baked) block is not re-highlighted`, `a comment on a runtime-highlighted code block survives reload`; source: `assets/js/` - `highlightCodeBlocks()` |
| CMH-HL-03 | The runtime tokenizer (`diffLangKnown` / `_HL_FAMILY` in `assets/js/26-highlight.js`) knows every language the author-time highlighter supports (`highlight_code.LANGUAGE_CONFIGS`), so a supported language authored raw is always highlightable at runtime (via CMH-HL-01 and the diff highlighter) and can never silently render monochrome. A drift guard fails the moment an author-time language is added without runtime coverage. | `tests/test_highlight_parity.py` - `test_runtime_knows_every_author_time_language`; source: `assets/js/26-highlight.js` - `_HL_FAMILY` |
| CMH-KQL-01 | A KQL block is a framed `figure.cmh-kql` with `figcaption.cm-skip`, title, `Run in Azure Data Explorer` link, and commentable `pre > code.language-kusto`. | `tests/17-kql.spec.js` - framed KQL figure, safe ADX link in `cm-skip` chrome; `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_kql_figure_without_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_with_run_link_is_clean` (validator rejects a `figure.cmh-kql` missing a `cmh-kql-run` link) |
| CMH-KQL-02 | `tools/kusto/kusto_link.py` builds deterministic ADX deep links by normalizing line endings, gzip-compressing with mtime 0, base64-encoding, percent-encoding, and validating the cluster host. | `tests/test_kusto_link.py` - roundtrip, golden, URL shape, deterministic gzip, host validation, database encoding, stdin and CLI tests |
| CMH-KQL-03 | `tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py` highlights KQL at author time, escapes markup, keeps `textContent` equal to the raw LF-normalized query, and can emit code-only or full figure output. The `--code-only` mode stamps the `data-cmh-kql-no-cluster` metadata marker on the `<pre>` so a bare (non-runnable) block passes CMH-KQL-08; the full figure (with a cluster/run link) does not carry the marker. | `tests/test_kql_highlight.py` - exact text roundtrip, markup escaping, token class, CLI, golden renderer, and `test_render_code_no_cluster_marks_pre` tests; `tests/test_coverage_gaps.py` - `test_kql_render_block_emits_full_commentable_figure` |
| CMH-KQL-04 | Highlighted KQL code stays selectable and commentable; comments over highlighted spans survive reload; token colors and the `Run in Azure Data Explorer` link stay AA-legible on dark KQL surfaces in document dark theme and deck mode. | `tests/17-kql.spec.js` - highlighted query commentable, comment survives reload, dark token colors; `tests/62-deck-regressions.spec.js` - `CMH-DECK-13: showcase deck code, KQL, and diff blocks keep readable contrast` |
| CMH-KQL-05 | The validator warns when a `cmh-kql-run` link is not an HTTPS ADX link or opens a blank target without `rel="noopener"`. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - Kusto run-link valid, wrong origin warning, and missing noopener warning tests |
| CMH-KQL-06 | The KQL caption title is a `cm-skip` click-to-copy button for the cluster name via `data-cmh-copy`; no separate cluster chip is emitted. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `the Kusto caption cluster name copies to the clipboard`; `tests/27-ui-batch3.spec.js` - `the KQL caption title copies the cluster name`; source: `tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py` - `render_block()` |
| CMH-KQL-07 | A framed KQL figure (`figure.cmh-kql`) MUST carry a real `cmh-kql-run` "Run in Azure Data Explorer" `<a>` link built with `tools/kusto/kusto_link.py`. The validator errors (non-zero exit in the default, non-strict mode) when a framed figure has no such link - detected by an actual `<a>` element carrying the `cmh-kql-run` class token, so query text that merely mentions `cmh-kql-run` does not satisfy it - or when a present run link's href, after HTML-entity decoding and URL parsing, is not `https` with host exactly `dataexplorer.azure.com` (a `javascript:`/`data:`/non-ADX or look-alike-host href is a hard error, not a warning). Bare (unframed) KQL blocks are governed separately by CMH-KQL-08. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_kql_figure_without_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_run_link_only_in_query_text_errors_missing`, `test_kql_figure_javascript_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_data_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_http_non_adx_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_lookalike_host_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_entity_encoded_javascript_run_link_errors`, `test_kql_figure_with_run_link_is_clean`; `tests/test_validate_cli.py` - `test_kql_figure_missing_run_link_exit_1` (CLI exit 1 in default mode) |
| CMH-KQL-08 | Every KQL code block must be runnable OR explicitly marked clusterless. A bare `<pre><code class="language-kusto">` (or `language-kql`) that is NOT inside a `figure.cmh-kql` carrying a Run in Azure Data Explorer link (CMH-KQL-07) is a hard validator error UNLESS the `<pre>` carries the explicit `data-cmh-kql-no-cluster` metadata marker, declaring there is genuinely no cluster to run it on. Authors should PREFER providing a cluster (`tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py <cluster> <database> <title>` builds the runnable figure); the marker is the rare escape hatch (`tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py --code-only` stamps it). The `<pre>` inside a valid figure is not double-flagged. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_bare_kusto_without_no_cluster_marker_errors`, `test_bare_kusto_with_no_cluster_marker_is_clean`, `test_bare_kusto_inside_kql_figure_is_not_double_flagged`; `tests/test_kql_highlight.py` - `test_render_code_no_cluster_marks_pre`, `test_cli_code_only`, `test_main_code_only_arg`; source: `tools/validate/checks/layer.py` - `_check_kql_blocks`, `tools/kusto/kql_highlight.py` - `render_code` |
| CMH-CHART-01 | Chart.js charts use a canvas inside `figure.chart` or `.cmh-chart`; the wrapper may stay `cm-skip` for text offsets, while the canvas is indexed by the image layer and saved as `imageKind: "chart"`. | `tests/24-copy-sort.spec.js` - `a chart canvas is commentable like an image`; `tests/25-example.spec.js` - `the chart is commentable and mermaid gantt/flowchart nodes are commentable (served over http)`; `tests/test_validate_charts.py` - canvas wrapper and valid fixture tests |
| CMH-CHART-02 | Chart data lives in valid JSON script blocks outside `#commentRoot`; JSON escapes `<`; chart init runs after the `END: commentable-html - JS` marker and after the Chart.js loader. | `tests/test_validate_charts.py` - script breakout, HTML comment breakout, invalid JSON, init before JS end, init before loader tests |
| CMH-CHART-03 | The default Chart.js recipe is self-contained: vendor/self-host Chart.js next to the HTML or inline it, keep loading synchronous, guard init with `typeof Chart === "undefined"`, and reserve CDN loading for explicit opt-in with pinned version plus SRI/crossorigin because it breaks the self-contained guarantee. | `tests/test_validate_charts.py` - loader, SRI, defer/async/module, pinned URL, accessibility, guard, and missing init tests; `../pkg/skills/commentable-html/references/charts-embedding.md` |

| CMH-CHART-06 | Commenting a chart canvas rings it with a visible highlight: the canvas gets `cm-img-hl` with a real outline, matching the image highlight (canvas selectors are covered by the CSS, not just `img`). | `tests/35-taxi-example.spec.js` - `commenting a chart canvas rings it with a visible highlight (CMH-CHART-HL-01)` |
| CMH-CHART-07 | The shipped examples exercise multiple chart kinds (bar, doughnut, pie, single-line, and dual-axis line) and complex mermaid (flowchart, sequence, gantt, state, class, ER, pie, and flowcharts with subgraphs), each rendering correctly; chart canvases and mermaid nodes are commentable via the image and mermaid layers. | `tests/35-taxi-example.spec.js` - `the added pie and line charts render as their intended kinds (CMH-TAXI-CHARTS-01)`, `the enriched subgraph flowchart and the sequence diagram both render (CMH-TAXI-MERMAID-01)`, `the chart and mermaid pipeline nodes are commentable (served over http)`; `tests/25-example.spec.js` - `the chart is commentable and mermaid gantt/flowchart nodes are commentable (served over http)` (subgraph clusters render); `tests/test_examples.py` - `test_new_showcase_examples_cover_triage_and_visuals`; `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `expanded demo item kinds convert to Markdown` |
| CMH-DEMO-01 | The shipped live-demo example set includes a triage-board report with a `data-cm-widget` kanban board plus a visuals report covering flowchart, sequence, gantt, state, class, ER, and pie mermaid diagrams, bar/line/pie/doughnut Chart.js charts, a code-review diff, and a KQL block. Each report carries a unique `data-comment-key`, validates cleanly, and exports its new item kinds to Markdown. | `tests/test_examples.py` - `test_new_showcase_examples_cover_triage_and_visuals`, `test_examples_have_unique_comment_keys`, validation/version tests; `tests/43-export-markdown.spec.js` - `expanded demo item kinds convert to Markdown` |
| CMH-DEMO-02 | Every shipped `examples/report-*.html` has a companion example-prompt file `examples/prompt-<name>.md` (community garden, taxi, triage, and metrics), and each prompt file carries the standard headings (`# Example prompt`, `## Prompt`, `## What you get`) plus a non-empty blockquote prompt that would generate that report. | `tests/test_examples.py` - `ExamplePromptTests.test_every_report_has_a_companion_prompt_file` |
| CMH-DEMO-03 | Each shipped example report (community garden, taxi, triage, and metrics) is exercised end to end by the interaction suite - commenting on prose, commenting on a widget/chart/mermaid part, clicking UI controls, and a seeded randomized monkey pass - served over http with no uncaught page error and with comments surviving a reload. | `tests/25-example.spec.js`, `tests/35-taxi-example.spec.js`, `tests/30-monkey.spec.js` (garden and taxi); `tests/53-more-examples.spec.js` - `commenting on prose and a widget part works and survives reload`, `clicking UI controls and a randomized monkey pass never crash` (triage and metrics) |
