| CMH-DEMO-05 | An editable-notes demo report (`report-notes.html`) ships with a single-line note and a multi-line note; it validates cleanly under `--strict`, has a companion prompt, uses a unique comment key, and its note round-trips end to end (edit, Copy all, cement, reopen). | `tests/test_examples.py` - `NotesExampleTests`; `tests/62-notes.spec.js` - `CMH-NOTE-E2E: reviewer edit -> Copy all -> notes_apply.py -> reopen shows the cemented source` |
| CMH-CHART-08 | Chart figures contain their canvases under narrow widths: `figure.chart` clips overflow, `.chart-wrap` has bounded height, canvases size to the wrapper, and Chart.js defaults/generated configs use `responsive: true` with `maintainAspectRatio: false`. | `tests/35-taxi-example.spec.js` - doughnut chart narrow-viewport containment; `tests/test_chart_block.py` - emitted config forces bounded responsive options; source: `assets/css/`, `tools/blocks/chart_block.py` |
| CMH-RESP-01 | On narrow screens, mermaid blocks and chart figures stay inside the content column and expose horizontal scrolling inside their own boxes, so wide diagrams and chart labels remain readable without overflowing the viewport; the test fixture must include at least one block whose `scrollWidth` strictly exceeds its `clientWidth` and can scroll horizontally. | `tests/51-charts-mobile.spec.js` - `charts and mermaid blocks are contained in the mobile content column (CMH-RESP-01)` |
| CMH-RESP-02 | Floating Add Comment and highlight bubbles respect the visible bounds of horizontally clipped rich-content containers (`pre.mermaid`, `figure.chart`, tables, and raw diffs): a fully clipped target hides the bubble, and a partly visible target clamps the bubble inside the container instead of floating over unrelated content. | `tests/51-charts-mobile.spec.js` - `chart add buttons stay inside the chart scroll container on mobile (CMH-RESP-02)`, `the highlight bubble hides and clamps to a horizontal-overflow clip container across container types (CMH-RESP-02)`, `the chart Add button clamps inside a narrow chart container on horizontal overflow (CMH-RESP-02)` |
| CMH-MMDLOAD-01 | The skill does not load mermaid itself; host pages should vendor/self-host mermaid for self-contained reports or consciously opt into a CDN that renders by default. The comment layer no-ops if mermaid never renders. | `tests/23-coverage.spec.js` - `CMH-MMDLOAD-01: mermaid renders by default when served over http` (CDN path now has E2E coverage); `tests/12-features.spec.js` verifies comment flows stay usable under denied external requests; `tests/04-comments-code-mermaid.spec.js` exercises locally vendored mermaid; manual for CDN opt-in guidance |
| CMH-MMDLOAD-02 | The mermaid CDN import is gated on the presence of a `pre.mermaid` / `div.mermaid` element, so a diagram-free document (including a deck) makes no external network request at all; a document that contains a diagram still loads mermaid and renders it. | `tests/40-widget-comments.spec.js` - `CMH-MMDLOAD-02: a diagram-free document makes no mermaid CDN request; a diagram loads it` |
| CMH-NET-01 | The commentable review layer is bundled inside the document, while optional rich content is an explicit CDN choice: mermaid, Chart.js, and any tooltip library may fetch from a CDN by default and should be self-hosted or inlined for fully self-contained sharing. When CDN assets are left remote, that content depends on the network and may not render identically when the file is shared. | source/docs: `assets/js/` core runtime; `../pkg/skills/commentable-html/references/mermaid-diagrams.md`; `../pkg/skills/commentable-html/references/charts-embedding.md` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-01 | `Export Offline` is available in both the overflow menu and sidebar. It builds the Portable export with current unhandled embedded comments, snapshots rendered mermaid blocks as inline SVG, snapshots chart canvases as PNG data images, removes automatic remote loaders and remote media beacons, declares the real `commentableHtmlLayer.mode` as `offline`, preserves the CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 region markers and `data-cmh-content-root`, keeps chart/mermaid anchors commentable, and produces a strict-valid file that renders with external network blocked. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline snapshots mermaid and Chart.js charts for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)`; `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - handled export filtering |
| CMH-OFFLINE-02 | `currentDocState()` treats CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 descriptor mode `offline`, or legacy offline chart snapshots without that descriptor, as the first-class Offline state when no unsaved comments or widget moves are present; the sidebar and toolbar badges show Offline after a zero-network reopen. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - reloaded offline export badge assertion in `Export Offline snapshots mermaid and Chart.js charts for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-03 | Editing and re-saving an already-offline document preserves CMH-FWDCOMPAT-01 descriptor mode `offline`, does not introduce remote refs, and re-running Export Offline keeps the offline chart snapshots stable. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `editing an already-offline document preserves offline mode and offline export is idempotent (CMH-OFFLINE-03)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-04 | Offline export stripping is data-safe and egress-safe: it skips comment-data and layer-descriptor JSON scripts even when comment text mentions `import('https://...')`, strips runnable inline scripts with remote dynamic imports, bare remote imports, remote `from` imports, or network dynamic-import targets, ignores `data-id="commentableHtmlLayer"` descriptor decoys when retargeting mode, neutralizes every network-loading media path (`img`, `iframe`, `video`, `audio`, `source`, `track`, SVG `image`/`use`, `input[type=image]`, `object`, `embed`, and legacy `background`), removes remote form `action` and `formaction` targets, removes refresh meta tags and remote preload/prefetch/prerender links, strips network CSS `@import` and `url(...)`, preserves benign inline scripts whose dynamic import comments contain `//` but no network literal, preserves custom canvas scripts that are not chart snapshots, and preserves only data and relative media references. Same-origin absolute URLs are stripped because an offline file must not beacon to its original host. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline snapshots mermaid and Chart.js charts for zero-network reopen (CMH-OFFLINE-01, CMH-OFFLINE-02)`, `Export Offline adds a zero-network CSP and strips loader, media, CSS, and event-handler egress (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`, `Export Offline neutralizes form posts and preserves safe canvas scripts (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)` |
| CMH-OFFLINE-05 | Offline exports add a restrictive Content Security Policy meta tag as defense in depth: inline runtime/styles and data images/fonts are allowed, all network egress, form posts, and embedding are blocked through `connect-src`, `form-action`, `frame-src`, `frame-ancestors`, and related directives, event-handler attributes are removed, and a reopened offline file with injected dynamic imports makes zero HTTP(S) requests. | `tests/49-offline-export.spec.js` - `Export Offline adds a zero-network CSP and strips loader, media, CSS, and event-handler egress (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`, `Export Offline neutralizes form posts and preserves safe canvas scripts (CMH-OFFLINE-04, CMH-OFFLINE-05)`; source: `assets/js/` - `_ensureOfflineCsp()`, `_stripOfflineEventHandlers()` |
## Robustness

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-ROBUST-01 | Random, seeded interaction (add, edit, delete, and clear comments in any order; sort, collapse, expand, copy, scroll, toggle syntax, hover mermaid and chart) never throws an uncaught error, never loses runtime-ready, and holds every invariant: no duplicate ids, count equals stored, DOM marks equal stored comments, and byte-for-byte anchor restore across reload. | `tests/08-noise.spec.js` - seeded add/edit/delete/clear invariants and byte-for-byte reload stability; `tests/30-monkey.spec.js` - full chrome plus render fuzz on kitchen-sink and on the example (mermaid plus chart) asserts no pageerror or console error and comment persistence |

## Persistence, security, and accessibility

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-PERSIST-01 | The document's `localStorage` namespace is `data-comment-key` for the JSON comment array, plus the sidecar keys `<key>::deleted` (embedded-delete tombstones), `<key>::commentSort`, `<key>::diffLayout`, `<key>::diffSyntax`, `<key>::tableSort`, `<key>::cl` (checklist state), and `<key>::note` (editable-notes state). Invalid JSON or storage failures degrade to empty/default state; only a primary comment-save failure surfaces to the reader. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `persistence sidecar keys are written under the document key (CMH-PERSIST-01)`, `invalid localStorage JSON degrades to defaults without breaking startup (CMH-PERSIST-01)`, `primary comment save failure stays usable but warns that persistence failed (CMH-PERSIST-01)`; source: `assets/js/` - `saveComments()`, per-feature `localStorage` helpers |
| CMH-PERSIST-02 | `data-comment-key` is origin-scoped and must be unique per document; reusing it intentionally shares comments, tombstones, and UI state, and changing it starts a new namespace with no automatic migration. `new_document.py --key auto` derives a stable key from the document IDENTITY (`--key-from-source`, then `--source`, then the `--out` path), never from `--label`, so two documents that merely share a label do not collide; with no identity source it requires an explicit `--key`. | source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py` - `resolve_key()`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_resolve_key_auto_same_label_distinct_identity_does_not_collide`, `test_resolve_key_auto_without_identity_requires_explicit_key`, `test_key_auto_derives_stable_key_from_out_path`, `test_key_auto_without_identity_exits_2` |
| CMH-PERSIST-03 | `mergeCommentSets()` de-dupes ids WITHIN the persisted array as well as across the embedded set, keeping the latest by timestamp (`updatedAt` then `createdAt`) regardless of array order, so a corrupted `localStorage` array that repeats an id renders and re-persists the newest version of that comment once, never twice. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `duplicate persisted comment ids dedupe and the newest-timestamp entry wins (CMH-PERSIST-03)`, `the newest duplicate wins even when it appears first (CMH-PERSIST-03)`; source: `assets/js/` - `mergeCommentSets()` |
| CMH-SEC-01 | Trust boundary: authored content fragments are trusted HTML and are NOT sanitized by `new_document.py` or the runtime; callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML before wrapping it. The runtime escapes or `textContent`s all user comment text and metadata, validates comment ids against `SAFE_ID_RE`, and escapes `<` as `\u003c` in the `embeddedComments` JSON. Validator coverage is targeted and does not make arbitrary host HTML safe. The rule is documented in `SKILL.md` (the "Trust boundary (MUST)" note) and in `new_document.py --help` (the epilog). **[partial - the "callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML" rule is documented and enforced only by convention; there is no automated sanitization in the validator or tooling; only the user-data escaping and id sanitization are tested]** | source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/new_document.py`; `tests/test_validate_self_contained.py` - embedded-item id tests; `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - diff raw-HTML tests; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_help_documents_the_trust_boundary` |
| CMH-A11Y-01 | Help and confirm dialogs expose `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, an accessible name, a Tab focus trap, Escape/backdrop close where applicable, and focus restoration to the trigger. | `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - Help dialog focus-trap and focus-restore; `tests/19-clear-confirm.spec.js` - confirm dialog role/trap/restore |
| CMH-A11Y-02 | The comment composer has an accessible name, a labelled textarea (not placeholder-only), deterministic initial focus to the textarea, Ctrl/Cmd+Enter save, Escape cancel, and focus restoration to the element that opened it. | `tests/33-coverage-gaps.spec.js` - composer focus-return, group semantics, labelled textarea, blank-note invalid; source: `assets/js/` - `createComposerElement()`, `closeComposerElement()` |
| CMH-A11Y-03 | Toasts carry `role="status"` or `role="alert"` and `aria-live` so screen readers announce them. | `tests/36-a11y.spec.js` - `toasts carry a live-region role so screen readers announce them` |
| CMH-A11Y-04 | The confirm dialog's Tab handler always calls `preventDefault` and, when focus has escaped the two buttons, pulls it back to the default (Cancel), so Tab can never reach the page behind the modal. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `the confirm dialog always traps Tab and pulls escaped focus back to Cancel (CMH-A11Y-04)`; source: `assets/js/` - `showConfirm()` |
| CMH-A11Y-05 | The composer textarea placeholder documents the save shortcut as `Ctrl/Cmd+Enter`, matching the handler that accepts both `ctrlKey` and `metaKey`, so Mac users see the correct key. | `tests/47-hardening.spec.js` - `the composer placeholder documents the Ctrl/Cmd+Enter save shortcut (CMH-A11Y-05)`; source: `assets/js/` - `createComposerElement()` |
| CMH-A11Y-06 | The disclosure toggles name the element they control via `aria-controls`: the overflow-menu trigger `#btnToolbarMenu` (`aria-haspopup`) points at `#toolbarMenu`, and the comments-panel toggle `#btnToggleSidebar` (`aria-expanded`) points at `#sidebar`, so assistive tech can associate each toggle with its target. | `tests/20-ui-chrome.spec.js` - `the sidebar/menu toggles declare the element they control via aria-controls (CMH-A11Y-06)`; source: `assets/template.shell.html` |
| CMH-A11Y-07 | The layer honors `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: a global CSS reset clamps every non-essential animation and transition (duration, delay, and multi-iteration loops) to a near-instant single pass, and every programmatic smooth scroll (jump-to-comment, scroll-to-top/bottom, heading deep-link, widget/checklist/notes jumps) routes through a shared `cmScrollBehavior()` helper that returns `auto` under the preference (because a JS `behavior` option overrides the CSS `scroll-behavior`), so motion-sensitive readers get an instant jump. Without the preference, animations keep their authored duration and scrolls stay smooth. Durations are clamped (not removed) so keyframe animations that set a final state still land. The deck slide stage additionally carries the vendored slide engine's own `prefers-reduced-motion` rule (animations instant, a minimal essential slide transition), which is out of scope for this layer reset. | `tests/67-reduced-motion.spec.js` - `reduced-motion clamps a keyframe animation to near-zero, normal keeps it (CMH-A11Y-07)` (duration, delay, iteration-count), `reduced-motion also clamps transitions (CMH-A11Y-07)` (duration, delay), `programmatic smooth scrolling becomes instant under reduced motion (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `programmatic smooth scrolling stays smooth without the preference (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `no layer JS partial hardcodes behavior:"smooth" ... (CMH-A11Y-07)`, `cmScrollBehavior() fails closed to "auto" when the preference cannot be determined (CMH-A11Y-07)`; source: `assets/css/95-reduced-motion.css`, `assets/js/00-preamble.js` - `cmScrollBehavior()` |
| CMH-TOC-06 | Generated heading ids are ASCII word-character slugs de-duped against existing ids; a heading with no slug content (e.g. non-Latin) falls back to `section`, `section-2`, and so on, so authors should supply explicit ids for stable non-Latin anchors. | `tests/38-spec-gaps.spec.js` - `browser heading ids fall back to ASCII section slugs for non-Latin headings (CMH-TOC-06)`; `tests/test_generate_toc.py`; source: `assets/js/`; `tools/authoring/generate_toc.py` |
| CMH-PRINT-01 | Flat documents have a direct print/PDF contract: print media hides runtime chrome (toolbar, sidebar, composers, add-comment controls, highlight bubbles, copy buttons, side TOC, footer, dialogs, and toasts), expands collapsed sections and notes, resets fixed or shadowed layout chrome into a readable paper flow using the `--cp-*` theme tokens, and materializes a non-`cm-skip` end-of-document comments appendix from the in-memory comments on `beforeprint` / print media entry. With JavaScript disabled, a print-only fallback states that live comments require JavaScript to render. Deck print keeps each `.slide` visible and page-broken one slide per page. | `tests/68-print.spec.js` - `CMH-PRINT-01: flat print hides runtime chrome, expands content, and materializes comments`, `CMH-PRINT-01: deck print keeps one slide per page` |

## Build pipeline, validator invariants, and tools

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-BUILD-01 | The canonical sources are the numbered topic partials under `assets/css/NN-topic.css` and `assets/js/NN-topic.js`, plus `assets/template.shell.html`. `build.py` assembles the layer CSS and runtime JS by DIRECTORY SORT of those partials (no hand-maintained order list; adding a partial does not edit build.py), concatenating them byte-for-byte and rstripping the final newline once; the sort order is load-bearing (the JS single-IIFE statement order, the CSS cascade). Sources must not be recombined into a `commentable-html.js`/`.css` monolith, and `dist/PORTABLE.html` and `dist/` are generated and should not be hand-edited. | `tests/test_build.py` - generated files match disk, idempotence, inline template roundtrip; `tests/test_assets_split.py` - `test_assembled_js_is_one_arrow_iife`, `test_preamble_partial_is_first_and_captures_the_snapshot`, `test_ordered_parts_are_sorted_nonempty_named_and_numeric`, `test_every_partial_ends_with_a_single_newline`, `test_ordered_parts_rejects_a_stray_unnumbered_file`, `test_exactly_one_js_partial_declares_cmh_version`, `test_build_all_reads_the_passed_assets_dir_not_the_default`, `test_the_monolith_sources_do_not_exist`; `README.md` |
| CMH-BUILD-07 | Every source partial is documented in a sibling `MODULES.md` that maps it to the SPEC feature-id areas it implements, so per-module coverage is auditable. The map is an enforced gate: every partial on disk has exactly one row (no undocumented or stale row), and every SPEC area a module claims is real and test-backed (a matching `AREA-NN` row exists in this spec and names a covering test). | `tests/test_module_coverage.py` - `test_js_modules_map_to_real_tested_spec_areas`, `test_css_modules_map_to_real_tested_spec_areas`; sources: `assets/js/MODULES.md`, `assets/css/MODULES.md` |
| CMH-BUILD-08 | `dev/SPEC.md` is a generated artifact assembled from directory-sorted `dev/spec/NN-*.md` topic partials by `dev/tools/build_spec.py`; `--check` compares the checked file to a fresh assembly and fails on drift, so concurrent spec edits land in topic partials instead of the monolith. | `tests/test_build_spec.py` - `BuildSpecTests`; `scripts/test_rebuild_all.py` - `test_check_runs_build_spec_screenshots_and_site_in_order_with_check_flag`; CI: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` runs `build_spec.py --check` |
| CMH-BUILD-02 | `dev/VERSION` is the single source of truth for the version; `build.py` reads it and stamps the runtime `CMH_VERSION` const, `pkg/plugin.json`, the marketplace entry, and each document's `<meta name="commentable-html-version">`. Companion filenames are version-agnostic, so a version bump does not rename dist files. `dist/manifest.json` records SHA-256 and byte size per companion and is serialized with sorted keys for a deterministic, diff-stable payload. | `tests/test_build.py` - version single sourced, manifest hashes, `test_manifest_is_deterministically_key_sorted`, single declaration tests |
| CMH-BUILD-03 | NonPortable output is materially smaller than inline output and avoids re-emitting boilerplate while still validating cleanly. | `tests/test_build.py` - nonportable size and both templates validate clean tests; `tests/07-nonportable.spec.js` nonportable tests |
| CMH-BUILD-04 | Generated fixtures and examples are kept fresh against current assets and exercise the feature surface. Each shipped `examples/report-*.html` is a pure build artifact assembled by `build.py` from its INDEPENDENT content source in `dev/examples/src/report-*.html`: `build.py` swaps in the current CSS/COMMENT UI/JS layer and re-stamps the `<meta>` version, and `--check` compares the shipped file to a fresh assembly, so a hand-edit or a stale/clobbered committed example - of its CONTENT as well as its layer - fails the build (closing the self-sourced hole the site pages had before #91; content edits must be made in `dev/examples/src/`). `build.py --check --check-fixtures` additionally runs the Playwright fixtures' `generate.mjs --check` (skipped only when node is absent), so the required `dist-in-sync` gate also owns fixture freshness (a version bump that leaves a fixture stale fails the fast gate, not only the slow `plugin-tests` job). | `tests/10-more.spec.js` - `committed sample fixtures are not stale (generate.mjs --check)`; `tests/test_examples.py` - example exists, validates, inlines images, exercises every feature, `test_examples_embed_current_version`, `test_build_check_catches_example_drift`, `test_build_check_catches_example_content_drift`, `test_build_check_flags_an_orphaned_example_with_no_source`; `tests/test_build.py` - `test_check_subprocess_passes`, `test_check_fixtures_passes_and_is_reported` |
| CMH-BUILD-05 | Generated documents carry no leading "Commentable HTML - TEMPLATE / DEMO" documentation header comment: the shipped `dist/PORTABLE.html` and `dist/NONPORTABLE.html`, every document produced by `new_document.make_document()`, and every shipped `examples/report-*.html` are free of that guidance (the region/content-root contract lives in the skill references instead), so a real report is never mislabeled as a template or demo. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - `NoTemplateDemoHeaderTests` (`test_shipped_dist_templates_carry_no_header`, `test_generated_document_carries_no_header`); `tests/test_examples.py` - `ExampleNoTemplateHeaderTests.test_examples_carry_no_template_header` |
| CMH-BUILD-06 | No shipped document bakes the transient runtime UI-state class `sidebar-open` (or the other transient body classes) into the `<body>` open tag: the canonical `assets/template.shell.html`, the generated `dist/PORTABLE.html` and `dist/NONPORTABLE.html`, every document produced by `new_document.make_document()`, and every shipped `examples/report-*.html` ship a plain `<body>`. `sidebar-open` is a transient state the layer toggles on `document.body`; baking it in renders the content full width with an empty sidebar gutter (the `body.sidebar-open .app` rule) before the runtime re-derives the state on load. | `tests/test_build.py` - `test_dist_templates_do_not_bake_sidebar_open_body_class`, `test_template_shell_does_not_bake_sidebar_open_body_class`; `tests/test_new_document.py` - `test_generated_document_does_not_bake_sidebar_open_body_class`; `tests/test_examples.py` - `ExampleNoSidebarOpenBodyTests.test_examples_do_not_bake_sidebar_open_body_class` |
| CMH-VAL-01 | `tools/validate/validate.py` checks region count and order, one real `#commentRoot`, valid JSON state blocks, unique required ids, globally unique layer JSON ids across all element types, JS close-tag safety, and `--cp-*` theme variables. | `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - required ids, regions, state blocks, comment root, CSS variable, scoped hidden, and mermaid tests |
| CMH-VAL-02 | The validator catches retrofit mistakes where the demo root survives a customized title or real content is accidentally placed inside an HTML comment, without treating a commented `data-id="commentRoot"` as a hidden real root. | `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - handled and embedded JSON block tests |
| CMH-VAL-03 | The validator warns for retired Export/Import UI (the note cites the exact removal version, "removed before the 1.0.0 release"), unscoped `[hidden]`, missing scoped hidden rule, missing `data-doc-label` or `data-doc-source`, and mermaid blocks missing `cm-skip`. | `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - content-root hook and demo leftover tests |
| CMH-VAL-04 | The validator enforces that `offsetWithin()` uses `normalizeBoundary()` so selections starting or ending on element boundaries can anchor. | `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - normalizeBoundary required, satisfied, and comment/string false-positive tests |
| CMH-VAL-05 | NonPortable validation checks local companion refs, existing files, assets registry warning, version meta, missing-asset banner, watchdog, http(s) or protocol-relative remote URL rejection, non-`file` scheme rejection, local `file://` refs, and absolute-path warning. A `?query`/`#fragment` cache-buster on a companion ref (e.g. `commentable-html.js?v=1.7.0`) is stripped before the suffix detection and the on-disk existence check, matching what the browser fetches. `validate()` accepts an optional `base_dir` to resolve companion refs against the file's final location, or `None` to check structure only when placement is deferred. `new_document.py` defaults to absolute `file://` companion refs, while `--assets-relative`, `--copy-assets`, and `--assets-href` keep their explicit modes. | `tests/test_validate_nonportable.py` - `NonPortableTests` companion, meta, banner, watchdog, missing file, parent/subfolder, remote URL, protocol-relative URL, non-file scheme, absolute path, `test_file_url_companion_refs_validate_clean`, `test_nonportable_detection_accepts_cache_busted_refs`, `test_cache_busted_companion_refs_validate_clean` tests; `tests/test_new_document.py` - NonPortable default file URL, `--assets-relative`, copy-assets, and assets-href generation tests |
| CMH-VAL-06 | Chart validation runs automatically when a canvas exists and can also run chart-only or layer-only from the CLI. | `tests/test_validate_charts.py` - chart validator tests; `tests/test_validate_cli.py` - unified validate and CLI flag tests |
| CMH-VAL-07 | The validator covers raw diff markup errors, Kusto run-link warnings, and mermaid `cm-skip` advisories. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - diff raw HTML and Kusto run-link tests; `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - mermaid `cm-skip` tests |
| CMH-VAL-08 | The validator enforces the self-contained guarantee on the finished document: an `<img>`/`<script>`/`<iframe>` that loads over the network is an error (Chart.js CDN is an exempt opt-in in portable mode), a fetching external `<link>` is a warning in portable mode, non-fetching remote links such as canonical and alternate metadata are ignored, and a local-path `<img>` warns to inline it. For descriptor mode `offline`, every network-loading script, fetching link, image, iframe, media source, form target, meta refresh redirect, CSS `@import`, and CSS `url(...)` is an error with no Chart.js exemption, the restrictive offline CSP is required, and data URLs remain valid. `<a href>` navigation is exempt. | `tests/test_validate_self_contained.py` - `NewCheckTests` external img/script/link, data-URI, chartjs-exempt, local-path, offline Chart.js rejection, offline network resources, offline CSP, offline form actions, offline meta refresh, offline CSS URLs, canonical-link pass, and offline inlined resource tests |
| CMH-VAL-09 | The validator reports every offending instance in one pass (no early exit): each raw-HTML diff block, each unlabeled `<canvas>` counted together, each `embeddedComments` item with a missing/unsafe id, and duplicate heading ids. `--strict` exits non-zero when any warning remains, so a single run surfaces everything to fix. | `tests/test_validate_kql.py` - `test_diff_block_reports_every_bad_block_in_one_pass`; `tests/test_validate_self_contained.py` - `NewCheckTests` canvas-report-all, embedded-item-id, duplicate-heading, and `--strict` tests |
| CMH-VAL-10 | The validator errors when the REAL parsed `<body>` open tag carries a transient runtime UI-state class (`sidebar-open`, `cm-sidebar-resizing`, or `cm-widget-dragging`): those are toggled on `document.body` at runtime and must never persist in a shipped file (a baked `sidebar-open` renders the content full width with an empty sidebar gutter via the `body.sidebar-open .app` rule before the layer re-derives state on load). The check reads the parsed `<body>` element's attributes (not a raw first-token regex), so a `<body class="sidebar-open">` decoy inside a `<head>` script or comment before the real body is ignored, a real dirty body after such a decoy is still caught, and a legitimate CSS/JS reference to `sidebar-open` is not flagged. | `tests/test_validate_kind.py` - `test_sidebar_open_body_class_errors`, `test_sidebar_open_only_in_css_or_js_is_clean`, `test_sidebar_open_decoy_before_real_body_is_clean`, `test_sidebar_open_real_body_after_decoy_errors` |
| CMH-SYN-01 | `tools/validate/validate.py` fails a mermaid sequenceDiagram whose message contains a `;` that splits it into a broken second statement. Once the first `;`-segment is a real signal, the ONLY valid tails are another full signal, a keyword-led statement, or an empty (trailing `;`) tail; ANY other tail - an arrow-without-colon signal, a bare word, or ordinary prose (e.g. `A->>B: hi; take entities + dict bags as-is`) - is flagged, which mermaid rejects as "Syntax error in text". Calibrated to zero false positives: only a NON-keyword-led, non-comment, non-empty tail that is not itself a valid signal is flagged (so a `participant A as "a->b"` alias, a keyword tail, and a valid multi-signal `A->>B: x; C->>D: y` are never flagged); runs as a content check inside `validate()`. | `tests/test_validate_mermaid.py` - `MermaidSequenceRule` (`test_flags_original_bug`, `test_valid_multi_signal_not_flagged`, `test_arrow_in_message_without_semicolon_not_flagged`, `test_participant_alias_with_arrow_not_flagged`, `test_semicolon_then_keyword_not_flagged`, `test_bare_word_tail_is_flagged`, `test_prose_tail_repro_is_flagged`), `MermaidWiredIntoValidate.test_validate_flags_broken_sequence_in_document` |
| CMH-SYN-02 | The Python mermaid checker deep-checks ONLY `sequenceDiagram`; every other diagram family (flowchart, graph, class, state, er, gantt, pie, ...) is recognized but delegated to the repo-side real-parser oracle, so it is never a Python false positive - including constructs earlier heuristics mis-flagged: a flowchart label containing `%%` or a literal `"` in a slash/round/hex shape, or a `click` callback. Within `sequenceDiagram` it stays zero-false-positive by only inspecting a line whose first `;`-segment is a real signal (never a keyword-led directive such as `accTitle:`/`accDescr:`, matched even with no space after the colon and even when its free text contains a post-arrow colon), only flagging non-keyword-led tails (so a `participant`/`activate`/`Note`/`link`/`accTitle:` tail is fine), and neutralizing `%%{init}%%` directives and mermaid character entities (`#59;`, `#quot;`) before the split while treating a line or `;`-segment that starts with `%%`, a single `%`, or `#` as a comment that consumes the rest of the line (mid-line `%`/`#` is literal, and an entity like `#quot;` is decoded first so an entity-led tail is still inspected). Unknown types and already-rendered `<svg>` blocks are skipped, and an empty block is flagged (it renders as mermaid's "No diagram type detected" error). | `tests/test_validate_mermaid.py` - `MermaidFlowchartDelegatedToOracle.test_flowchart_never_flagged`, `MermaidSequenceRule` (`test_acctitle_directive_not_flagged`, `test_inline_init_directive_not_flagged`), `MermaidRound2Regressions` (`test_acctitle_post_arrow_colon_not_flagged`, `test_acctitle_no_space_not_flagged`, `test_numeric_entity_semicolon_not_flagged`, `test_named_entity_semicolon_not_flagged`, `test_midline_percent_before_colon_not_flagged`, `test_keyword_tail_link_not_flagged`, `test_still_flags_the_real_bug`, `test_comment_tail_after_semicolon_not_flagged`), `MermaidRound5Regressions` (`test_percent_comment_tail_not_flagged`, `test_hash_comment_tail_not_flagged`, `test_comment_leader_consumes_rest_of_line`, `test_linestart_single_comment_not_flagged`, `test_entity_led_tail_is_still_flagged`, `test_still_flags_the_real_bug`), `MermaidTypeGating` (`test_other_types_not_flagged`, `test_rendered_block_skipped`, `test_empty_block_is_flagged`) |
| CMH-SYN-03 | The shipped Python mermaid checker has ZERO false positives, proven against a broad (200+ entry) corpus spanning every mermaid diagram family (`tests/fixtures/mermaid-corpus.json`, bulk sources in `mermaid-corpus.src.json`): each entry's `valid` is stamped by the REAL mermaid parser and its `py_flag` by the REAL Python checker, and `build_mermaid_corpus.mjs` FAILS generation if the checker flags any parser-valid diagram, so the checker never flags a diagram the real parser accepts. The Python test asserts a valid entry is never flagged (and that real bugs are still caught); unknown/unhandled diagram types and already-rendered (`<svg>`) blocks are skipped. | `tests/test_validate_mermaid.py` - `MermaidCorpusDifferential` (`test_no_false_positives_and_catches_flagged`, `test_every_flagged_entry_is_really_invalid`), `MermaidTypeGating` (`test_other_types_not_flagged`, `test_rendered_block_skipped`, `test_empty_block_is_flagged`) |
| CMH-SYN-04 | A repo-side real-parser oracle (`dev/tools/validate_render.mjs`, DEV ONLY, never shipped) loads the same mermaid and Chart.js the runtime uses in its own headless browser and validates every mermaid diagram and Chart.js config in the shipped example reports, so the repo cannot ship a diagram or chart that renders as a syntax-error bomb; it also flags an unknown Chart.js type (via the controller registry). Extraction uses the browser's DOMParser (authoritative for HTML comments, unquoted attributes, and nested tags - e.g. a mermaid `<pre>` inside a wrapper `<div>`), so no diagram is silently skipped - including an empty/whitespace-only `<pre class="mermaid">` host, which the real parser rejects as "No diagram type detected" and the oracle therefore flags rather than dropping. The Playwright gate shells out to the standalone tool (launching a browser inside the test runner deadlocks). | `tests/60-diagram-syntax.spec.js` - `CMH-SYN-04: every mermaid diagram and chart in the shipped example reports parses with the real parser`, `CMH-SYN-04: the oracle extracts diagrams robustly (nested div, unquoted attr, comment) and flags a broken one`, `CMH-SYN-04: the oracle flags an empty mermaid host instead of silently skipping it` |
| CMH-SYN-05 | The differential corpus is kept honest in CI: `build_mermaid_corpus.mjs --check` regenerates every `valid` label from the REAL mermaid parser and every `py_flag` from the REAL Python checker and FAILS on any false positive (the checker flags a parser-valid diagram) or drift, so the Python checker's zero-false-positive guarantee can never rest on a stale label. | `tests/60-diagram-syntax.spec.js` - `CMH-SYN-05: the differential corpus is in sync with the real parser and Python checker (no false positives)` |
| CMH-SYN-06 | `tools/validate/validate.py` fails an embedded `<script type="application/json">` block (other than the layer's own state blocks) that is empty or not valid JSON - including a `NaN`/`Infinity` literal that the browser's `JSON.parse` rejects, or a raw `</script>` that truncates the block into invalid JSON - so a data block whose `JSON.parse()` would throw at runtime is caught. When a `<canvas>` is present AND the chart checks run, they own JSON validity so it is reported exactly once; in `--layer-only` mode the chart checks do not run, so this module still validates. | `tests/test_validate_json.py` - `JsonBlockValidity` (`test_invalid_json_no_canvas_is_flagged`, `test_valid_json_not_flagged`, `test_empty_block_flagged`, `test_layer_blocks_ignored`, `test_canvas_present_defers_to_chart_checks`, `test_non_json_script_ignored`, `test_wired_into_validate_module`, `test_nan_infinity_flagged`, `test_script_breakout_is_caught_via_json_validity`, `test_layer_only_still_checks_json_when_canvas_present`) |
| CMH-SYN-07 | The content-syntax checks live in the sibling `tools/cmhval/` package, which ships alongside `validate.py`. If it cannot be imported (a broken/partial install), the validator fails CLOSED rather than silently passing: `check_mermaid_syntax` errors when the parser captured any mermaid block and `check_json_blocks` errors when a non-layer `application/json` data block is present (deferring, as the real check does, when a canvas lets the chart path own JSON validity). A document with no such content still validates, and `--charts-only` (which uses `check_charts`, not `cmhval`) is unaffected; `validate._CMHVAL_AVAILABLE` records whether the real package loaded. | `tests/test_validate_failclosed.py` - `CmhvalImportFailClosed` (`test_import_failure_is_recorded`, `test_mermaid_check_fails_closed_when_content_present`, `test_mermaid_check_passes_clean_document`, `test_json_check_fails_closed_when_data_block_present`, `test_json_check_ignores_layer_state_blocks`, `test_json_check_defers_to_chart_path_when_canvas_present`, `test_json_check_passes_clean_document`) |
| CMH-VAL-11 | The validator warns when a `<pre><code class="language-XXX">` block declares a HIGHLIGHTABLE language (one `tools/blocks/highlight_code.py` supports, resolving its aliases like `cs`->`csharp`) but carries no `cmh-code-*` token spans, i.e. it was authored with a language label but never run through the highlighter, so it renders as monochrome text. Only BLOCK code (a `<code>` inside a `<pre>`) is checked; an inline `<code class="language-...">` in prose, a non-highlightable label (`language-text`, `language-kusto`, ...), and an empty block are all skipped, and an already-highlighted block passes. The single source of truth for "is this highlightable" is `highlight_code.LANGUAGE_CONFIGS`/`ALIASES`. | `tests/test_validate_highlighting.py` - `test_unhighlighted_language_code_block_warns`, `test_unhighlighted_alias_language_code_block_warns`, `test_highlighted_code_block_is_clean`, `test_non_highlightable_language_code_block_is_clean`, `test_code_block_without_language_is_not_flagged`, `test_inline_language_code_is_not_flagged`; source: `tools/validate/checks/highlighting.py` - `check_code_highlighting()` |
| CMH-VAL-12 | The validator warns when a non-mermaid `<pre>` or `<pre><code>` block directly carries `cm-skip` inside the live content region, because that would make the code non-commentable; a host-chrome `pre.cm-skip` outside `#commentRoot` and a bare `pre.mermaid.cm-skip` remain valid. | `tests/test_validate_highlighting.py` - `test_normal_pre_cmskip_warns_CMH_VAL_12`, `test_normal_pre_code_cmskip_warns_CMH_VAL_12`, `test_host_chrome_pre_cmskip_is_not_flagged_CMH_VAL_12`; `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - `test_mermaid_with_cmskip_ok` |
| CMH-VAL-13 | The validator errors when the live `#commentRoot` uses the documentation example key `data-comment-key="my-doc"` while still allowing the sanctioned commented-out documentation example. | `tests/test_validate_layer.py` - `test_active_my_doc_key_is_error_CMH_VAL_13`, `test_doc_example_commented_root_is_ok` |
| CMH-VAL-14 | The validator emits a non-fatal WARNING when a `report`, `plan`, or `generic` document has two or more top-level `<h2>` headings that are direct children of `#commentRoot` with no `<section>` wrapper, so the author restores the boxed section-card layout (`#commentRoot > section`). A document whose `<h2>`s sit inside `<section>` wrappers (so they are not direct children) does not warn, a single top-level `<h2>` does not warn, and `slides` / `board` kinds are exempt. | `tests/test_section_wrapping.py` - `test_flat_top_level_h2_warns`, `test_sectioned_content_is_clean`, `test_slides_kind_is_exempt`, `test_single_top_level_h2_is_clean` |
| CMH-VAL-15 | The validator emits a non-fatal WARNING when a `report` or `plan` section is a wall of prose: a run of `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LONG` (4) or more consecutive long paragraphs (past a tunable `MIN_LONG_PARAGRAPH_CHARS` length) inside `#commentRoot` with no table, list, figure, diff, chart, or diagram breaking it up, nudging the author toward a real skimmable layout. It uses a dedicated stack-based HTMLParser density pass (not the single-pass token stream), is scoped to `#commentRoot`, ignores `cm-skip` subtrees and paragraphs nested inside layout blocks (an INLINE `cm-skip` inside a paragraph excludes only its own text and does not split it), tracks sections as a heading stack so nested and headless sections are labeled by their own heading and each distinct wall (a run of consecutive long paragraphs) is reported - two walls in the same section separated by a layout block or a nested section are both reported, anchors the `<section>` run-break to a genuinely matched frame (a stray `</section>` never suppresses a wall), resets the run at any layout block / heading / `<section>` boundary (and at a `cm-skip` block), and runs ONLY for `report` and `plan` (`slides`, `board`, `generic`, and a missing or unknown kind do not emit this advisory). The thresholds are tunable via `check_density(html, min_chars=, max_run=)`. | `tests/test_density.py` - `test_cmh_val_15_prose_wall_warns`, `test_cmh_val_15_layout_bearing_section_is_clean`, `test_cmh_val_15_short_paragraphs_are_clean`, `test_cmh_val_15_heading_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_slides_and_board_are_exempt`, `test_cmh_val_15_cm_skip_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_val_15_content_outside_root_is_ignored`, `test_cmh_val_15_threshold_is_tunable`, `test_cmh_val_15_paragraphs_inside_layout_do_not_count`, `test_cmh_val_15_unclosed_paragraphs_still_warn`, `test_cmh_val_15_generic_and_missing_kind_are_exempt`, `test_cmh_val_15_first_kind_meta_wins`, `test_cmh_val_15_whitespace_does_not_inflate_length`, `test_cmh_val_15_short_paragraph_breaks_consecutiveness`, `test_cmh_val_15_cm_skip_block_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_self_closing_layout_breaks_the_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_headless_section_is_not_mislabeled`, `test_cmh_val_15_inline_cm_skip_does_not_split_a_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_block_cm_skip_breaks_run_after_unclosed_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_phrasing_cm_skip_does_not_split_a_paragraph`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_flat_top_level_walls_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_section_in_layout_block_does_not_reframe`, `test_cmh_val_15_min_chars_is_tunable`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_headless_walls_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_two_walls_in_one_section_are_both_reported`, `test_cmh_val_15_phrasing_cm_skip_between_paragraphs_breaks_run`, `test_cmh_val_15_prose_after_nested_section_uses_outer_heading`, `test_cmh_val_15_stray_close_section_does_not_suppress`, `test_cmh_val_15_wired_into_validate`; source: `tools/validate/checks/density.py`, wired in `tools/validate/validate.py` |
| CMH-KIND-01 | Every document declares its kind in a mandatory `<meta name="commentable-html-kind">` whose content is one of `report`, `plan`, `slides`, `board`, or `generic`. The validator errors when the meta is missing or the kind is unknown, and enforces that title-bearing kinds (`report`, `plan`) carry a TOP-LEVEL `<h1>` as a direct child of `#commentRoot` (a lede-wrapped `<header class="cmh-lede"><h1>` counts) while `slides`, `board`, and `generic` do not; an `<h1>` nested only inside a deeper `<section>` does not satisfy the rule and is an error, and a top-level lede whose `<h1>` is empty (no non-empty title text) is also an error rather than passing on the lede wrapper class alone. | `tests/test_validate_kind.py` - `test_missing_kind_meta_errors`, `test_unknown_kind_errors`, `test_report_without_h1_errors`, `test_plan_without_h1_errors`, `test_report_with_h1_is_clean`, `test_report_with_nested_only_h1_errors`, `test_report_with_lede_wrapped_h1_is_clean`, `test_report_with_empty_lede_errors`, `test_slides_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_board_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_generic_without_h1_is_clean`, `test_kind_is_case_insensitive` |
| CMH-KIND-02 | `new_document.py` and `retrofit.py` require `--kind`, stamp the `commentable-html-kind` meta, and only auto-add a document title from `--label` for kinds that carry one (`report`/`plan` add it when the fragment has none; `slides`/`board` do not). `retrofit.py` detects an existing kind meta by parsing head metadata order-independently (including a non-canonical `content`/`name` attribute order) and REPLACES it in place rather than appending a duplicate, so retrofitting `--kind` onto a host that already declares a kind yields exactly one meta with the requested kind. `new_document.DOC_KINDS` stays in sync with `validate._DOC_KINDS`. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - `KindTests` (`test_doc_kinds_match_validate`, `test_kind_is_required`, `test_kind_stamps_meta`, `test_slides_kind_does_not_add_title`, `test_report_kind_adds_title_and_validates`, `test_make_document_stamps_kind`); `tests/test_retrofit.py` - `test_kind_is_stamped_and_required`, `test_existing_kind_meta_is_replaced_not_duplicated`, `test_reordered_existing_kind_meta_is_replaced` |
| CMH-KIND-03 | `upgrade.py` migrates a document that predates the kind meta by adding a default `generic` kind, so upgrading an older document yields one that declares a kind and validates. | CMH-HL-02 | `tools/blocks/highlight_document.py` bakes syntax highlighting into every raw, language-labelled `<pre><code class="language-XXX">` block of a file in one pass (aliases resolved, e.g. `cs` -> `csharp`), rewriting only blocks whose language the highlighter supports and whose inner is still raw (no `cmh-code-*` spans, no markup); an already-highlighted block, an inline `<code>`, a non-highlightable label, and an empty block are left untouched, and re-running is a no-op. It exposes a `--check` mode (exit 1 if any block needs highlighting) and a `-` stdin/stdout mode. `tools/authoring/finalize.py` runs this step by DEFAULT (opt out with `--no-highlight`) before validation, so the standard finalization bakes highlighting. | `tests/test_highlight_document.py` - full unit suite; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_highlights_code_blocks_by_default`, `test_no_highlight_flag_skips_highlighting`; source: `tools/blocks/highlight_document.py`, `tools/authoring/finalize.py` |

| CMH-STAMP-02 | `validate.py` (and `finalize.py`) write a `<meta name="commentable-html-validated">` timestamp into the file ONLY on a STRICT-CLEAN pass (no errors AND no warnings), so the validated stamp means the document actually passed strict validation. `--no-stamp` keeps a pure/CI run read-only (no write); a doc with a warning is not stamped. | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `test_validate_stamps_validated_on_clean_file`, `test_validate_no_stamp_flag_leaves_file_unstamped`, `test_validate_does_not_stamp_a_doc_with_warnings`; `tests/test_finalize.py` - `test_stamps_validated_on_a_strict_clean_finalize`, `test_no_stamp_flag_skips_the_validated_stamp`; source: `tools/validate/validate.py` - `_stamp_validated_file`, `tools/authoring/finalize.py` |

A strict-validated document, and any document with no created stamp (older/foreign), show nothing. The banner is `cm-skip` chrome added to `CMH_INJECTED_CHROME`, so it never bakes into a Save/Export snapshot and is re-derived on load. This is a last resort; the skill MUST always finalize and strict-validate. | `tests/64-validation-banner.spec.js` - `an unvalidated document shows the fallback banner`, `a validated document shows no banner`, `a document with no created stamp shows no banner`, `the banner is dismissible`, `the banner does not leak into a Plain export`; source: `assets/js/38-validation-banner.js`, `assets/css/87-validation-banner.css` |
| CMH-STAMP-04 | The document-producing tools stamp the creating AI agent's session id as `<meta name="commentable-html-session-id">` (and its agent slug as `commentable-html-agent`) so the footer can copy it (CMH-FOOT-04). `new_document.py` and `deck_scaffold.py` stamp it BY DEFAULT from `--session-id` or, when that is absent, an auto-detected environment variable (`COPILOT_AGENT_SESSION_ID` -> copilot, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`/`CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` -> claude); `--agent` overrides the slug and `--no-session-id` opts out. When several agents' session ids are visible at once (a nested launch), the agent that is the immediate runtime wins via its marker env var (`CLAUDECODE` vs `COPILOT_CLI`). The session stamp is idempotent (a re-scaffold preserves the original producer) and a blank id is a no-op. | `tests/test_doc_stamp.py` - `DocStampUnitTests` (`test_stamp_session_sets_id_and_agent`, `test_stamp_session_is_idempotent`, `test_stamp_session_blank_is_noop`, `test_detect_session_reads_copilot_env`, `test_detect_session_reads_claude_env`, `test_detect_session_reads_claude_legacy_env`, `test_detect_session_prefers_copilot_when_both_present_and_no_runtime_marker`, `test_detect_session_prefers_the_running_agent_when_both_present`, `test_detect_session_absent_is_none`), `NewDocumentSessionStampTests`; `tests/test_deck_scaffold.py` - `test_scaffold_stamps_session_from_flag`, `test_scaffold_stamps_session_from_environment_by_default`, `test_scaffold_no_session_id_flag_suppresses`; source: `tools/authoring/doc_stamp.py`, `tools/authoring/new_document.py`, `tools/deck/deck_scaffold.py`; local live checks (not CI; drive the real agent CLIs, shared core `tests/_session_e2e.py`): `tests/copilot_e2e_check.py`, `tests/claude_e2e_check.py` |

| CMH-TOOL-07 | `tools/authoring/new_document.py` produces a **NonPortable** document by default (companion refs repointed to the skill's `dist/` by a relative path, or `--copy-assets` to copy them next to the file, or `--assets-href` for a custom prefix) and a single self-contained file with `--portable`; it replaces only the CONTENT fragment and the `#commentRoot` data attributes, refuses demo keys (except when re-stamping an existing document via `allow_reserved_key`), updates the `<title>`, reads a fragment from a file or UTF-8 stdin, and self-validates before writing. NonPortable output to stdout (no `--out`) is refused unless `--assets-href` is given, because bare companion names on a stream have no on-disk folder to resolve against; the caller must supply `--assets-href`, `--out` (optionally with `--copy-assets`), or `--portable`. | `tests/test_new_document.py` - clone, content swap, demo-key rejection, allow-reserved-key, active-root-attrs, stdin, title, NonPortable default/`--copy-assets`/`--assets-href`, `test_nonportable_stdout_is_refused`, `test_nonportable_stdout_ok_with_assets_href`, `test_nonportable_stdout_ok_when_portable`, `--portable`, mode mutual-exclusion, and validation tests |
| CMH-TOOL-08 | `tools/authoring/upgrade.py` upgrades a standalone document by replacing only the CSS, COMMENT UI, and JS regions (preserving handled ids, embedded comments, content, and the root), validates the result in a temp file, and leaves the target unchanged if validation fails or the validator crashes. A region boundary is matched only on a COMPLETE marker line (the keyword phrase alone on its line, optionally wrapped by its comment delimiter and/or a `====` banner, with nothing else on the line), so an inline mention or a line-leading marker phrase carrying trailing authored text cannot corrupt a swap. The shipped upgrader, maintainer build tool, and runtime export slicers reject duplicate BEGIN or END markers for every layer region instead of silently slicing through authored content. | `tests/test_upgrade.py` - region swap, preservation, stale `--check`, validation-failure-leaves-target-unchanged, validator-crash-surfaced, marker-line tests, duplicate BEGIN/END tests; `tests/test_build.py` - marker-line and duplicate BEGIN/END tests; `tests/06-save-export.spec.js` - export region-slicer coverage; `tests/52-region-guard.spec.js` - runtime Export Plain and Export Offline abort on a duplicated EMBEDDED COMMENTS BEGIN (F28) or END (F37) marker and download nothing |

| CMH-TOOL-13 | `tools/blocks/chart_block.py` emits a validator-clean `figure.chart` + CDN-failure-guarded Chart.js loader + chart init script from a Chart.js spec JSON; `<` is escaped as `\u003c` in the spec JSON; `aria-label` is derived from title and caption; invalid canvas ids and non-object specs are rejected at the CLI boundary. | CMH-TOOL-14 | `tools/authoring/finalize.py` runs TOC generation, section-wrapping, fix-skip, inline-images, highlighting, ordered-list TOC number de-dup (CMH-TOC-10), and the report/plan document-overview strip (CMH-STATS-01, opt out `--no-stats`) in a fixed deterministic order then validates; each optional step is enabled or disabled by its own flag; with no step flags only the default steps and validation run; `--strict` promotes warnings to errors; exits non-zero on any error. | `tests/test_finalize.py` - step order, no-step-flags validation-only, per-step enable, doc-stats default/opt-out/non-card, toc-number de-dup, warnings without/with strict, errors both modes, and clean-template tests |
| CMH-TOOL-15 | `tools/authoring/retrofit.py` injects the full commentable layer into an existing unlayered HTML file, using parsed `<head>` and `<body>` insertion points, a CSS-only theme-vars block, a unique non-demo key, the content-root descriptor contract, optional `--root-selector "#id"`, matching NonPortable asset options, `--portable`, and `--skip-selectors`; it accepts valid HTML5 optional-end-tag closures while rejecting malformed non-optional closures, keeps JS outside the CONTENT markers, avoids nested `<main>` by wrapping with `<div>` when the body already has a main element, refuses already-layered or ambiguous documents, self-closing non-void roots, and implicitly closed roots, ignores fake container tags inside comments or scripts, warns on likely CSS collisions, validates before writing, and leaves the target unchanged when validation fails. | CMH-TOOL-HELP-01 | Every shipped CLI tool under `tools/<topic>/` (discovered recursively across the topic buckets; the `deck_common` shared library has no CLI and is excluded) answers `--help` and `-h` with exit 0 and prints usage text to stdout, so discovering a tool never treats the flag as a filename or fails. A tool that parses argv itself honors a `--` end-of-options separator: `-h`/`--help` is treated as a request for help only before a bare `--`, and any `-h`/`--help` after it is a positional data value (filename/query/id), not help. | `tests/test_cli_help.py` - `test_there_are_tools_to_check`, `test_every_tool_answers_help_with_exit_zero`, `test_help_flag_before_end_of_options_triggers_help`, `test_help_flag_after_end_of_options_is_data`, `test_dash_h_after_separator_is_treated_as_data_end_to_end` |
| CMH-TOOL-LAYOUT-01 | The shipped tools are grouped into per-topic buckets under `tools/<topic>/` (`deck`, `kusto`, `checklist`, `blocks`, `authoring`, `validate`); the `tools/` root holds only the `_toolpath.py` sys.path bootstrap. Each tool imports its siblings by bare name and resolves shipped resources (`dist/`, `vendor/`) through `tools/_toolpath.py`, which puts the tools root and every topic subdirectory on `sys.path` and exposes `SKILL_ROOT`, so a tool keeps working regardless of which bucket it lives in. Every expected tool exists at its bucket path, no tool is left flat at the `tools/` root or is unlisted, and every tool imports cleanly by bare name. | `tests/test_tools_layout.py` - `test_every_expected_tool_exists_at_its_bucket_path`, `test_tools_root_holds_only_the_bootstrap_and_buckets`, `test_no_shipped_tool_is_unlisted`, `test_every_tool_imports_by_bare_name` |

## Claude Code compatibility

commentable-html output is a portable HTML artifact that works with any agent, and the plugin is installable in Claude Code as well as the GitHub Copilot CLI. It ships a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` beside the Copilot `plugin.json` and is listed in the repo-root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (a subset mirror of `.github/plugin/marketplace.json`). The auto-updater is dual-agent too (its own `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and Claude `hooks/hooks.json`), so both published plugins install in either agent.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-CLAUDE-01 | The plugin is installable in Claude Code: it ships `pkg/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` whose identity fields (name, version, description, author, license, keywords) mirror the Copilot `pkg/plugin.json`, declares `"skills": "./skills/"` which resolves to the shipped skill, and appears in the repo-root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (whose entries mirror the matching `.github/plugin/marketplace.json` entries by name/version/source). `scripts/validate_claude_compat.py` checks this structurally and, when the `claude` CLI is present, runs `claude plugin validate --strict` on the marketplace and each Claude plugin dir. | `tests/test_claude_manifest.py` - `test_claude_plugin_manifest_ships`, `test_identity_fields_mirror_the_copilot_manifest`, `test_skills_path_resolves_and_skill_ships`; `scripts/test_validate_claude_compat.py` - `test_compatible_repo_has_no_errors`, the structural-failure tests, and `test_repo_manifests_are_claude_compatible` |

## Deck integration (frontend-slides)

The commentable-html skill incorporates the third-party frontend-slides design system (MIT, Zara Zhang) as NATIVE CMH deck theme presets: curated preset profiles under `pkg/skills/commentable-html/tools/deck/themes/` recolour the deck stage, components, and reusable recipe classes with system-font, contrast-safe tokens, so a themed deck needs no per-deck CSS and no per-deck hand-translation. A curated, pristine subset of the upstream skill is still vendored under `pkg/skills/commentable-html/vendor/frontend-slides/` (enforced pristine by a required CI gate) as design provenance and the maintainer refresh source, and to supply the inlined `viewport-base.css` and the PPTX extractor. The author-time deck tools live under `pkg/skills/commentable-html/tools/deck/`, and the runtime deck profile lives in the layer (activated only by `data-cmh-mode="deck"`, so non-deck documents are unaffected). See `references/deck-contract.md` for the runtime interface contract.

| Feature id | Behavior | Covering tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CMH-DECK-01 | The skill detects a presentation/deck request ("slide deck", "presentation", "pitch deck", "convert this ppt") and CONFIRMS with the user before building a real deck rather than silently producing a flat document; if the user declines it falls back to a normal flat document. This is an agent-behavior convention in `SKILL.md` ("Deck capability"), so its coverage is `manual`. | `manual` - documented in `SKILL.md`; agent-behavior convention, not automatable |
| CMH-DECK-02 | `deck/deck_scaffold.py` produces a valid, create-only, commentable-native fixed-stage deck: `#commentRoot` carries `data-cmh-mode="deck"` and a `commentable-html-kind` of `slides`, the body holds `.deck-viewport > .deck-stage` with `viewport-base.css` inlined, every slide is a `<section class="slide">` with a unique stable `data-slide-id` and the first is `.active`, the deck body has no inline editor / remote fonts / host script, ids are deterministic, and it refuses to overwrite an existing output without `--force`. Before writing it runs BOTH the base validator AND the deck contract (`deck_validate.deck_checks`) and fails closed (writing nothing) on any contract violation; its deck-mode injection targets the real content root (never the template decoy) idempotently. | `tests/test_deck_scaffold.py` - `test_scaffold_is_valid_and_commentable_native`, `test_slides_have_stable_ids_first_active`, `test_deck_body_has_no_editor_fonts_or_script`, `test_content_ids_preserved_and_missing_ones_minted`, `test_deterministic_ids`, `test_create_only_then_force`, `test_fragment_without_slides_errors`, `test_existing_ids_and_inject_targets_real_root`, `test_scaffold_fails_closed_on_remote_media`, `test_scaffold_declares_slides_kind`, `test_scaffold_has_legible_presentation_defaults` |
| CMH-DECK-03a | `deck/pptx_to_fragment.py` HTML-escapes extracted slide text (no `<script>` / `</b>` / `-->` injection; RTL and emoji preserved), emits one `<section class="slide">` per slide with a stable id, ignores speaker notes, and the local `--pptx` fallback fails closed without `python-pptx`. It also schema-validates the extracted JSON (each slide/content-block/image is an object; `content`/`images` are lists), rejects an empty slide list and a slide with no title/text/image, and vets every image `path` as local-relative OR a self-contained `data:image/...` URI (rejecting a remote/other scheme, `//host`, absolute, or `..` traversal; normalizing backslashes; skipping an empty path). The `--pptx` fallback inlines each extracted image as a `data:` URI before its temp dir is deleted, so a converted deck never ships a dangling `assets/...` reference. | `tests/test_pptx_to_fragment.py` - `test_hostile_text_is_escaped`, `test_notes_are_ignored`, `test_structure_and_stable_id`, `test_duplicate_slides_get_distinct_ids`, `test_cli_stdin_to_stdout`, `test_non_list_input_errors`, `test_pptx_fallback_fails_closed`, `test_remote_and_unsafe_image_paths_rejected`, `test_data_image_uri_is_accepted`, `test_local_pptx_inlines_extracted_images_as_data_uri`, `test_inline_local_images_leaves_odd_and_missing_paths`, `test_backslash_image_path_normalized`, `test_empty_image_path_skipped_not_error`, `test_empty_slide_list_rejected`, `test_slide_with_no_content_rejected`, `test_non_dict_slide_rejected`, `test_non_list_content_or_images_rejected`, `test_non_dict_content_block_or_image_rejected`, `test_cli_empty_list_exits_nonzero` |
| CMH-DECK-03b | When a `.pptx` conversion is requested, the flow prefers the Anthropic `pptx` skill (when installed) and falls back to the vendored local extractor. This is prompt-level routing in `SKILL.md`; only the deterministic escaping and the local fallback (CMH-DECK-03a) are automated, so this row's coverage is `manual`. | `manual` - prompt-level wording in `SKILL.md`; the deterministic extraction path is covered by CMH-DECK-03a |
| CMH-DECK-04 | `deck/deck_validate.py` passes a scaffolded deck and fails closed on each deck-contract violation. Active-content and egress checks are HTML-parser based (not regex), so they cannot be bypassed by a solidus attribute separator (`<svg/onload=>`), an entity-encoded scheme (`&#106;avascript:`), an unquoted attribute (`<img src=//evil>`), or an SVG `<image>`/`<use>` `href`. It rejects: missing `data-cmh-mode`, a remote font reference, remote media/resource (`img`/`video`/`audio`/`source`/`track`/`image`/`use`/`iframe`/`embed`/`object`/`link`/`base` fetch attr or CSS `url()` to `http(s):` / `//`), a remote CSS `@import`, an inline event handler, `<iframe>/<object>/<embed>`, an external `<script src/href>` or any `<script>` nested in `<svg>` (an inline chart-init `<script>` is allowed), a `<meta http-equiv=refresh>` redirect, a dangerous URL scheme (`javascript:`/`vbscript:`/`data:text/html`), a `../` asset reference, a `<deck-stage>` component, or a duplicate slide id. It tolerates a local-relative image, a `data:` image URI, an external hyperlink (`<a href>` is not egress), and a local `<link href>`; it does not false-positive on the plain-text token "edit-toggle" in prose. The content region is anchored on the full HTML-comment markers and uses the LAST end marker, so slide text containing the bare marker text cannot truncate validation. It also runs the base validator. | `tests/test_deck_validate.py` - `test_valid_deck_passes`, `test_missing_deck_mode_fails`, `test_remote_font_fails`, `test_event_handler_fails`, `test_iframe_fails`, `test_javascript_url_fails`, `test_parent_traversal_fails`, `test_deckstage_component_fails`, `test_duplicate_slide_id_fails`, `test_cli_passes_on_valid_deck`, `test_remote_media_fails`, `test_remote_css_url_fails`, `test_local_media_and_data_uri_pass`, `test_unquoted_javascript_and_traversal_fail`, `test_edit_toggle_plaintext_is_not_a_false_positive`, `test_solidus_and_whitespace_separated_event_handler_fails`, `test_entity_encoded_javascript_url_fails`, `test_unquoted_remote_media_fails`, `test_svg_image_and_use_remote_href_fails`, `test_html_image_alias_background_and_image_set_egress_fail`, `test_external_hyperlink_is_allowed`, `test_injected_end_marker_does_not_truncate_validation`, `test_begin_marker_without_close_reports_missing_region`, `test_duplicate_active_content_is_deduplicated`, `test_svg_and_external_scripts_fail_inline_chart_script_allowed`, `test_stray_svg_close_and_benign_meta_are_handled`, `test_remote_link_base_and_meta_refresh_fail_local_link_allowed` |
| CMH-DECK-18 | `deck/deck_fix_fonts.py` deterministically removes remote web-font loaders (`<link>`, remote `@import`, and remote `@font-face`) and rewrites deck font stacks to the approved system families: serif/editorial to Iowan, slab/display/script to Impact, geometric/body sans to system-ui, monospace to Cascadia, and explicit CJK remote families to a dropped system fallback. A fixed valid deck passes `deck_validate.py`. | `tests/test_deck_fix_fonts.py` - `test_cmh_deck_18_maps_web_fonts_and_strips_remote_loaders`, `test_cmh_deck_18_cli_writes_deterministic_output` |
| CMH-DECK-THEME-01 | A native deck theme preset is a named JSON profile under `tools/deck/themes/<name>.theme.json` of allowlisted deck CSS custom properties (system-font stacks + colors, optional local `data:` fonts). `deck/_deck_theme.py` loads it fail-closed - rejecting an unknown token, an unsafe value (reusing the shared `_brand_profile` value validators), a non-color value for a color token, and, at load time, a preset whose own declared `contrastPairs` fall below AA - and `render()` emits a single `<style id="cmh-deck-theme" class="cm-skip">` block scoped to `.deck-viewport/.deck-stage/.slide`. `deck_scaffold.py --theme <name>` injects the block into the content region (so the deck-contract and contrast checks cover it) and the deck still passes `deck_validate.py`; the shipped `terminal` preset (dark/technical, system monospace) is adapted from frontend-slides. | `tests/test_deck_theme.py` - `test_terminal_preset_ships_and_lists`, `test_render_emits_cm_skip_scoped_block_with_tokens`, `test_unknown_token_rejected`, `test_non_color_value_for_color_token_rejected`, `test_unsafe_value_rejected`, `test_preset_level_contrast_self_check_fails_closed`, `test_terminal_preset_pairs_pass_self_check`, `test_scaffold_theme_terminal_is_valid_and_themed`, `test_scaffold_unknown_theme_fails_closed`; `tests/70-deck-theme.spec.js` - `CMH-DECK-THEME-01: the terminal theme recolours the stage, slides, code, and tables` |
| CMH-DECK-THEME-02 | `deck/deck_theme.py apply` re-themes an existing deck in place, idempotently and comment-safely: it swaps ONLY the `cmh-deck-theme` block (inserting one after `cmh-deck-stage` if absent) and never touches slide markup, `data-slide-id` values, or the embedded-comments / handled-ids blocks. Because the block is `cm-skip` it carries no comment-offset text, so re-theming a commented deck to a different-length preset never shifts a stored anchor - a restored highlight keeps its original text on its original slide. The write is atomic and fails closed (source untouched) on a non-deck, an unknown preset, or a deck that does not validate; it refuses a repo example/source deck without `--force`, and `--list` prints the presets. | `tests/test_deck_theme_apply.py` - `test_apply_is_comment_safe_and_idempotent`, `test_retheme_to_different_length_preserves_slides_and_comment_blocks`, `test_non_deck_fails_closed`, `test_unknown_preset_fails_closed`, `test_out_writes_target_and_leaves_source_unchanged`, `test_refuses_repo_example_without_force`, `test_list_presets`; `tests/70-deck-theme.spec.js` - `CMH-DECK-THEME-02: re-theming a commented deck keeps every anchor on its original text and slide` |

It renders a fixed corpus of deck slide fragments (`dev/eval/corpus/*.html`) under an unthemed baseline AND every shipped preset via the real `deck_scaffold` -> `deck_validate` path, and records per (theme, corpus) cell: the create-time result (0 == deck-contract + AA-contrast pass), the `deck_checks` error count (must be 0), the `deck_warnings` content-overload advisory count (clipping proxy, must be 0), and the byte delta a theme adds over the baseline (token/latency proxy, bounded by a ceiling). `gate_failures()` turns these into pass/fail thresholds so a new preset that fails contrast, clips authored content, or bloats a deck fails the gate; the subjective "blind visual preference" leg is an explicit manual step (documented in `frontend-slides-upstream-sync.md`), not automated. | `tests/test_theme_eval.py` - `test_real_corpus_and_themes_pass_the_gate`, `test_every_shipped_theme_and_the_baseline_are_covered`, `test_each_theme_adds_a_bounded_nonzero_block`, `test_gate_catches_a_real_content_overload_slide`, `test_gate_catches_a_scaffold_failure`, `test_gate_catches_a_validator_error`, `test_gate_catches_theme_bloat`, `test_clean_rows_have_no_failures`, `test_cli_returns_zero_on_the_real_corpus`, `test_cli_fails_closed_on_an_empty_or_missing_corpus` |

Because the default component tokens are dark, a light preset overrides the full token set (slide + code + syntax + table head + diff + mermaid) and still passes `_deck_theme.load` (both the composited effective-contrast check over translucent diff rows and its own opaque `contrastPairs` self-check at AA), and the objective harness (CMH-DECK-THEME-05) renders both clean (no validator error, no clipping advisory, bounded byte delta). To keep light decks legible, the previously hard-coded-dark code-control surfaces are now themeable via `var(--token, <byte-exact default>)`: the code/KQL language + copy badge background (`--cmh-deck-code-badge-bg`), the KQL run-link hover colour (`--cmh-deck-code-link-hover-fg`), and the Mermaid subgraph cluster fill/stroke (`--cmh-deck-mermaid-cluster-fill`/`-stroke`); the light presets set readable values and self-check the badge and cluster-label pairs. | `tests/test_deck_theme.py` - `test_new_light_presets_ship_and_list`, `test_every_shipped_preset_loads_and_self_checks`, `test_new_presets_are_light`, `test_new_presets_carry_frontend_slides_provenance`, `test_new_presets_render_a_scoped_labelled_block`; `tests/test_theme_eval.py` - `test_every_shipped_theme_and_the_baseline_are_covered`, `test_real_corpus_and_themes_pass_the_gate` |
| CMH-DECK-05 | The runtime deck profile (activated by `#commentRoot[data-cmh-mode="deck"]` on the real content root) exposes `window.__cmhDeck` (`showSlide`/`showSlideById`/`activeSlideId`/`slideCount`), scales the fixed 1920x1080 stage to exactly `min(vw/1920, vh/1080)` (refit via a `ResizeObserver` on the viewport, so a window resize or comment-mode inset re-fits it), starts on the first slide and navigates by keyboard (Arrow/Space/PageUp/PageDown/Home/End, gated so a keypress inside an editable field - input/textarea/select/contenteditable/`.cm-skip` - never navigates) / id / a slide-oriented control bar (Prev, a live `N / total` counter with a spoken `aria-label`, Next, with WCAG-2.5.3 aria-labels and Prev/Next disabled at the boundaries), guards `showSlide` against a non-integer or out-of-range index and `showSlideById` against an empty/unknown id, dispatches `cmh:slidechange` only on a CHANGED active slide (never at setup, on a re-selection of the active slide, or on an invalid call), installs its chrome exactly once (idempotent) with the toggle first and the nav bar last in focus order, suppresses the flow-document chrome (heading anchors, collapsible carets, side TOC, footer, scroll progress), and defaults to a full-screen presentation (**present mode** hides the comment sidebar/toolbar and suppresses the selection popup AND the document context menu while preserving the native right-click); a **comment mode** toggle (stable accessible name + `aria-pressed`) reveals the sidebar, insets the stage so the sidebar does not overlap it (nav bar re-centred on the reduced stage), force-reveals animated content, and gates navigation keys; a comment card jumps to (activates) its owning slide (deck-aware jump, resolving text and non-text `[data-cids~]` anchors, and a document-wide comment jumps to slide 1) with highlights restoring on hidden slides across reload. The deck present-mode body class is stripped on export, and a deck survives Export Offline (mode, comments on hidden slides, and the card jump all round-trip with zero network). The sidebar's empty state carries a deck-specific hint on how to comment on a live slide; on a narrow screen the deck controls yield to the open comment sidebar. Non-deck documents are unaffected (the profile is fully gated on the deck signal). | `tests/52-deck.spec.js` - `CMH-DECK-05: exposes the controller and starts on the first slide`, `CMH-DECK-05d: keyboard, id, and prev/next navigation; doc-mode chrome suppressed`, `CMH-DECK-05d: typing in an editable field does not navigate; deck chrome is installed once`, `CMH-DECK-05d: the deck-aware jump resolves a non-text ([data-cids~]) anchor's slide`, `CMH-DECK-05d: cmh:slidechange fires only for a changed active slide`, `CMH-DECK-05: the stage refits on viewport resize and comment mode narrows it`, `CMH-DECK-05: slides fit the fixed 1080px stage and vertical overflow is detectable as clipping`, `CMH-DECK-05a: present mode hides the comment UI; comment mode reveals it and gates keys`, `CMH-DECK-05a: present mode suppresses the doc-comment menu and keeps the native menu; comment mode restores it`, `CMH-DECK-05a: on a narrow screen the deck controls yield to the open sidebar`, `CMH-DECK-05a: comment mode force-reveals staged slide content`, `CMH-DECK-05c/05d: a comment restores on a hidden slide and its card jumps to that slide`, `CMH-DECK-05c: a comment on a hidden slide restores after reload`, `CMH-DECK-05: a REAL scaffolded deck activates the runtime, not the flow-document chrome`, `CMH-DECK-05: Export Offline round-trips a deck (mode + comments + card jump) with zero network` |

It now also resolves computed descendant text color against the nearest authored background in the DOM (for cases such as a link whose `color` lives on the link but whose dark background lives on its caption container), and checks connector/arrow `stroke` contrast against the visible background with a 3.0:1 floor. `deck/deck_validate.py` applies it to the deck CONTENT region for explicit inline `style` pairs, CSS rules whose same declaration block sets `color` plus the last effective `background` or `background-color`, computed element/background pairs, connector strokes, and deck theme variable pairs (`--slide-fg` with `--slide-bg`/`--stage-bg`); errors name the selector, element, or variable pair and both colors so low-contrast authoring defects fail before publish. The tool pins the capture clock, random seed, viewport, locale, timezone, motion preferences, browser font rendering flags, CSS animation state, and capture fonts, then normalizes PNG output and judges freshness with a bounded pixel-diff tolerance so repeated captures stay visually stable on the same browser environment even when Chromium text rasterization jitters by a few pixels. Optional positional args `[example] [outDir] [prefix]` capture or check any example (the prefix is basename-sanitized), and `--print-paths` resolves the defaults without launching a browser. ## Coverage gaps

The following features are documented but lack full automated behavior coverage, or have a known mismatch between spec and documentation:

**Remaining manual or design-only gaps:**
- CMH-DECK-01: deck-intent detection and the "confirm before building a real deck" gate are an agent-behavior convention in `SKILL.md` ("Deck capability"), not an automated test.
- CMH-DECK-03b: the "prefer the Anthropic `pptx` skill when installed, fall back to the local extractor" preference is prompt-level wording in `SKILL.md`; only the deterministic escaping and the local fallback (CMH-DECK-03a) are automated.
- CMH-CONTENT-05: ADO resource links in authored content are a manual authoring convention with no validator or E2E enforcement.
- CMH-CONTENT-06: prose cross-reference links are only partially covered by TOC anchor tests.
- CMH-KQL-01: a framed KQL figure (`figure.cmh-kql`) is now hard-enforced to carry a Run in Azure Data Explorer link (CMH-KQL-07, a validator error). Bare, unframed `<pre>` KQL blocks are now also enforced (CMH-KQL-08): they must be marked `data-cmh-kql-no-cluster` when there is genuinely no cluster to run them on, so a missing cluster is a deliberate choice, not an accidental omission.
- CMH-CHART-05: chart recipe semantics, dark chart palette choices, and data hygiene rules are documentation and review conventions, not automated tests.
- CMH-WIDGET-18: widget drag suppresses text selection inside draggable cards by design; drag behavior is covered, but the selection-suppression decision itself is a manual UX contract.
- CMH-NET-01 / CMH-MMDLOAD-01: CDN opt-in and local vendoring choices are manual. CMH-MMDLOAD-01 now has E2E coverage for the default CDN path; manual guidance for vendor/self-host advice remains.

**Documentation/implementation mismatches (spec is correct; docs or tooling do not reflect it):**
- CMH-COPY-06: `references/limitations.md` implies all copy paths have the `prompt` fallback; only Copy all does. The per-block no-prompt rule now has E2E coverage, but the docs still need alignment outside this tests-only pass.
- CMH-SEC-01: the "callers must sanitize untrusted host HTML before wrapping" rule is stated in the spec and is now documented in `SKILL.md` (the "Trust boundary (MUST)" note) and `new_document.py --help`; it still has no automated enforcement in the validator or tooling.
